description_plain: Your child can use letters to stand for numbers in real problems and write and solve multi-step equations and inequalities to find answers.

subject: Math
description: Solve multi-step real-life and mathematical problems posed with positive and negative rational numbers in any form and assess the reasonableness of answers using mental computation and estimation strategies.
description_plain: Your child can solve multi-step problems with positive and negative numbers in different forms, using tools and checking if answers make sense.

subject: Math
description: Understand that rewriting an expression in different forms in a problem context can shed light on the problem and how the quantities in it are related.
description_plain: Your child understands that changing how an expression looks can help understand the problem and how the numbers relate.

subject: Math
description: Apply properties of operations as strategies to add, subtract, factor, expand and simplify linear expressions with rational coefficients.
description_plain: Your child can use math rules to add, subtract, factor, expand, and simplify expressions with rational numbers.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world and mathematical problems involving the four operations with rational numbers. (Computations with rational numbers extend the rules for manipulating fractions to complex fractions.
description_plain: Your child can solve real-life and math problems using addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division with rational numbers.

subject: Math
description: Convert between equivalent fractions, decimals, or percents.
description_plain: Your child can change fractions to decimals or percents and vice versa.

subject: Math
description: Convert a rational number to a decimal using long division; know that the decimal form of a rational number terminates in 0s or eventually repeats.
description_plain: Your child can turn a fraction into a decimal by dividing and knows that the decimal either ends or repeats.

subject: Math
description: Apply and name properties of operations used as strategies to multiply and divide rational numbers.
description_plain: Your child can use and name math rules to multiply and divide rational numbers.

subject: Math
description: Understand that integers can be divided, provided that the divisor is not zero, and every quotient of integers (with non-zero divisor) is a rational number. If p and q are integers, then –(p/q) = (–p)/q = p/(–q). Interpret quotients of rational numbers by describing real-world contexts.
description_plain: Your child understands that dividing whole numbers (except by zero) gives rational numbers and can explain real-life examples of dividing rational numbers.

subject: Math
description: Understand that multiplication is extended from fractions to rational numbers by requiring that operations continue to satisfy the properties of operations, particularly the distributive property, leading to products such as (–1)(–1) = 1 and the rules for multiplying signed numbers. Interpret products of rational numbers by describing real-world contexts.
description_plain: Your child understands how multiplication works with rational numbers, including negatives, and can explain real-world examples.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understandings of multiplication and division and of fractions to multiply and divide rational numbers and use equivalent representations.
description_plain: Your child can multiply and divide rational numbers by building on what they know about fractions.

subject: Math
description: Apply properties of operations as strategies to add and subtract rational numbers.
description_plain: Your child can use math rules to add and subtract rational numbers.

subject: Math
description: Understand subtraction of rational numbers as adding the additive inverse, p – q = p + (–q). Show that the distance between two rational numbers on the number line is the absolute value of their difference, and apply this principle in real-world contexts.
description_plain: Your child understands that subtracting a number is like adding its opposite and can use the distance between numbers on a number line in real situations.

subject: Math
description: Understand addition of rational numbers (p + q as the number located a distance |q| from p, in the positive or negative direction depending on whether q is positive or negative). Interpret sums of rational numbers by describing real-world contexts.
description_plain: Your child understands adding rational numbers as moving along a number line and can explain real-world examples of adding positive and negative numbers.

subject: Math
description: Show that a number and its opposite have a sum of 0 (additive inverses). Describe situations in which opposite quantities combine to make 0.
description_plain: Your child knows that a number plus its opposite equals zero and can describe situations where opposite amounts cancel out.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understandings of addition and subtraction to add and subtract rational numbers; represent addition and subtraction on a horizontal or vertical number line diagram.
description_plain: Your child can add and subtract rational numbers and show these operations on a number line.

subject: Math
description: Use proportional relationships to solve multistep ratio and percent problems.
description_plain: Your child can use proportional relationships to solve multi-step problems involving ratios and percents.

subject: Math
description: Understand the concept of unit rate and show it on a coordinate plane. Explain what a point (x, y) on the graph of a proportional relationship means in terms of the situation, with special attention to the points (0, 0) and (1, r) where r is the unit rate.
description_plain: Your child understands unit rates and can show them on a graph, explaining what points like (0,0) and (1, r) mean in the situation.

subject: Math
description: Represent proportional relationships by equations and multiple representations such as tables, graphs, diagrams, sequences, and contextual situations.
description_plain: Your child can show proportional relationships using equations, tables, graphs, diagrams, and real-life examples.

subject: Math
description: Identify the constant of proportionality (unit rate) in tables, graphs, equations, diagrams, and verbal descriptions of proportional relationships in real world situations.
description_plain: Your child can find the unit rate (constant of proportionality) in tables, graphs, equations, pictures, and descriptions of real-world proportional relationships.

subject: Math
description: Decide whether two quantities are in a proportional relationship (e.g., by testing for equivalent ratios in a table or graphing on a coordinate plane and observing whether the graph is a straight line through the origin).
description_plain: Your child can decide if two quantities are proportional by checking ratios in tables or graphs and seeing if the graph is a straight line through the origin.

subject: Math
description: Recognize and represent proportional relationships between quantities. Make basic inferences or logical predictions from proportional relationships.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and show proportional relationships and make simple predictions based on them.

subject: Math
description: Compute unit rates associated with ratios of fractions, including ratios of lengths, areas and other quantities measured in like or different units.
description_plain: Your child can find unit rates for ratios involving fractions, like speed or area, and write them as complex fractions or simpler numbers.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world and mathematical problems involving area, volume and surface area of two- and three-dimensional objects composed of triangles, quadrilaterals, polygons, cubes, and right prisms.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems about area, volume, and surface area of 2D and 3D shapes like triangles, polygons, cubes, and prisms.

subject: Math
description: Use facts about supplementary, complementary, vertical, and adjacent angles in a multi-step problem to write and solve simple equations for an unknown angle in a figure.
description_plain: Your child can use facts about different types of angles to write and solve equations to find unknown angles in shapes.

subject: Math
description: Know the formulas for the area and circumference of a circle and use them to solve problems; give an informal derivation of the relationship between the circumference and area of a circle.
description_plain: Your child knows the formulas for the area and circumference of a circle and can use them to solve problems and understand how they relate.

subject: Math
description: Describe the two-dimensional figures, i.e., cross-section, that result from slicing three-dimensional figures, as in plane sections of right rectangular prisms and right rectangular pyramids.
description_plain: Your child can describe the flat shapes made when slicing 3D shapes like rectangular prisms and pyramids.

subject: Math
description: Draw (freehand, with ruler and protractor, and with technology) geometric shapes including polygons and circles with given conditions. Focus on constructing triangles from three measures of angles or sides, noticing when the conditions determine a unique triangle, more than one triangle, or no triangle.
description_plain: Your child can draw shapes like polygons and circles using tools or technology, and can make triangles from given sides or angles, knowing when the triangle is unique or not possible.

subject: Math
description: Solve problems involving scale drawings of geometric figures, including computing actual lengths and areas from a scale drawing and reproducing a scale drawing at a different scale.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems using scale drawings, finding actual lengths and areas, and can redraw figures at different scales.

subject: Math
description: Look for and express regularity in repeated reasoning.
description_plain: Your child can notice and explain patterns or repeated steps when solving problems.

subject: Math
description: Look for and make use of structure.
description_plain: Your child can notice and use the way math problems are put together to help solve them.

subject: Math
description: Use appropriate tools strategically.
description_plain: Your child can choose and use the right tools, like calculators, rulers, or graphs, to help solve math problems.

subject: Math
description: Construct viable arguments and critique the reasoning of others.
description_plain: Your child can explain their math thinking and listen to and question the ideas of others.

subject: Math
description: Reason abstractly and quantitatively.
description_plain: Your child can think about math problems in different ways, using numbers and real-life examples.

subject: Math
description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them.
description_plain: Your child can understand what a math problem is asking and keep working until they find a solution.

subject: Math
description: Recognize a line of symmetry for a two-dimensional (plane) figure as a line across the figure such that the figure can be folded along the line into matching parts. Identify line-symmetric figures and draw lines of symmetry.
description_plain: Your child can find and draw lines of symmetry on flat shapes by folding them to see if the parts match.

subject: Math
description: Classify two-dimensional (plane) figures based on the presence or absence of parallel or perpendicular lines, or the presence or absence of angles of a specified size. Recognize right triangles as a category, and identify right triangles.
description_plain: Your child can sort flat shapes by looking for parallel or perpendicular lines and specific angle sizes, and can recognize right triangles.

subject: Math
description: Draw points, lines, line segments, rays, angles (right, acute, obtuse), and perpendicular, parallel, and intersecting line segments. Identify these in two-dimensional (plane) figures.
description_plain: Your child can draw and identify points, lines, line segments, rays, different types of angles, and lines that are perpendicular, parallel, or intersecting.

subject: Math
description: Compare two decimals to hundredths by reasoning about their size. Recognize that comparisons are valid only when the two decimals refer to the same whole. Record the results of comparisons with the symbols >, =, or <, and justify the conclusions (e.g., by using a visual model).
description_plain: Your child can compare two decimals to hundredths by thinking about their size, write >, =, or < to show the comparison, and explain why, using pictures or models.

subject: Math
description: Use decimal notation for fractions with denominators 10 or 100.
description_plain: Your child can write fractions with denominators of 10 or 100 as decimals.

subject: Math
description: Express a fraction with denominator 10 as an equivalent fraction with denominator 100, and use this technique to add two fractions with respective denominators 10 and 100.
description_plain: Your child can change a fraction with denominator 10 into an equal fraction with denominator 100, and use this to add fractions with denominators 10 and 100.

subject: Math
description: Solve word problems involving multiplication of a fraction by a whole number (e.g., by using visual fraction models and equations to represent the problem). Check for the reasonableness of the answer.
description_plain: Your child can solve word problems where a fraction is multiplied by a whole number, using pictures or equations, and check if the answer makes sense.

subject: Math
description: Understand a multiple of a/b as a multiple of 1/b, and use this understanding to multiply a fraction by a whole number.
description_plain: Your child understands that multiplying a fraction by a whole number means multiplying the numerator and can use this to find the product.

subject: Math
description: Understand a fraction a/b as a multiple of 1/b.
description_plain: Your child understands that a fraction like a/b means a times the size of 1/b.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understandings of multiplication to multiply a fraction by a whole number.
description_plain: Your child can multiply a fraction by a whole number using what they already know about multiplication.

subject: Math
description: Solve word problems involving addition and subtraction of fractions referring to the same whole and having like denominators (e.g., by using visual fraction models and equations to represent the problem).
description_plain: Your child can solve word problems that add or subtract fractions with the same denominator.

subject: Math
description: Add and subtract mixed numbers with like denominators (e.g., by replacing each mixed number with an equivalent fraction, and/or by using properties of operations and the relationship between addition and subtraction).
description_plain: Your child can add and subtract mixed numbers with the same denominator by changing them into fractions or using addition and subtraction rules.

subject: Math
description: Decompose a fraction into a sum of fractions with the same denominator in more than one way, recording each decomposition by an equation. Justify decompositions (e.g., by using a visual fraction model).
description_plain: Your child can break a fraction into a sum of smaller fractions with the same denominator in different ways and explain using pictures.

subject: Math
description: Understand addition and subtraction of fractions as joining and separating parts referring to the same whole.
description_plain: Your child understands adding and subtracting fractions as putting parts together or taking parts apart when they refer to the same whole.

subject: Math
description: Understand a fraction a/b with a > 1 as a sum of fractions 1/b.
description_plain: Your child understands that a fraction with numerator greater than 1 is the same as adding several fractions with numerator 1.

subject: Math
description: Compare two fractions with different numerators and different denominators (e.g., by creating common denominators or numerators, or by comparing to a benchmark fraction such as 1/2). Recognize that comparisons are valid only when the two fractions refer to the same whole. Record the results of comparisons with symbols >, =, or <, and justify the conclusions (e.g., by using a visual fraction model).
description_plain: Your child can compare two fractions with different numerators and denominators by making them easier to compare and explain which is bigger or smaller using >, =, or <.

subject: Math
description: Explain why a fraction a/b is equivalent to a fraction (n × a)/(n × b) by using visual fraction models, with attention to how the number and size of the parts differ even though the two fractions themselves are the same size. Use this principle to recognize and generate equivalent fractions.
description_plain: Your child can explain why two fractions are equal by using pictures that show how the parts are different sizes but the fractions are the same.

subject: Math
description: Recognize angle measure as additive. When an angle is divided into non-overlapping parts, the angle measure of the whole is the sum of the angle measures of the parts. Solve addition and subtraction problems to find unknown angles on a diagram in real world and mathematical problems (e.g., by using an equation with a symbol for the unknown angle measure).
description_plain: Your child understands that the size of a whole angle is the sum of its parts and can find missing angles by adding or subtracting.

subject: Math
description: Measure and draw angles in whole-number degrees using a protractor. Estimate and sketch angles of specified measure.
description_plain: Your child can measure and draw angles using a protractor and can estimate and sketch angles of given sizes.

subject: Math
description: An angle that turns through n one-degree angles is said to have an angle measure of n degrees.
description_plain: Your child knows that an angle made by turning through n one-degree steps has a measure of n degrees.

subject: Math
description: An angle is measured with reference to a circle with its center at the common endpoint of the rays, by considering the fraction of the circular arc between the points where the two rays intersect the circle. An angle that turns through 1/360 of a circle is called a "one-degree angle," and can be used to measure angles.
description_plain: Your child understands that angles are measured by how much of a circle they cover, with one degree being 1/360 of a full turn.

subject: Math
description: Recognize angles as geometric shapes that are formed wherever two rays share a common endpoint, and understand the following concepts of angle measurement:
description_plain: Your child knows that an angle is made where two rays meet and understands basic ideas about measuring angles.

subject: Math
description: Explain the classification of data from real-world problems shown in graphical representations including the use of terms range and mode with a given set of data. (L)
description_plain: Your child can explain data shown in graphs from real-life problems, including understanding terms like range and mode.

subject: Math
description: Make a line plot to display a data set of measurements in fractions of a unit (1/2, 1/4, 1/8). Solve problems involving addition and subtraction of fractions by using information presented in line plots.
description_plain: Your child can make a line plot to show measurements using fractions and solve problems by adding or subtracting those fractions.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world problems involving elapsed time between U.S. time zones (including Alaska Standard time). (L)
description_plain: Your child can solve real-world problems about elapsed time between different U.S. time zones.

subject: Math
description: Apply the area and perimeter formulas for rectangles in real world and mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child can use formulas to find the area and perimeter of rectangles in real-life and math problems.

subject: Math
description: Use the four operations to solve word problems involving distances, intervals of time, liquid volumes, masses of objects, and money, including problems involving simple fractions or decimals, and problems that require expressing measurements given in a larger unit in terms of a smaller unit. Represent measurement quantities using diagrams such as number line diagrams that feature a measurement scale.
description_plain: Your child can solve word problems using addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division with measurements like distance, time, volume, weight, and money.

subject: Math
description: Know relative sizes of measurement units within one system of units including km, m, cm; kg, g; lb, oz.; l, ml; hr, min, sec. Within a single system of measurement, express measurements in a larger unit in terms of a smaller unit. Record measurement equivalents in a two-column table.
description_plain: Your child knows how different units of measurement relate within the same system and can convert measurements from larger to smaller units and make a conversion table.

subject: Math
description: Find whole-number quotients and remainders with up to four-digit dividends and one-digit divisors, using strategies based on place value, the properties of operations, and/or the relationship between multiplication and division. Illustrate and explain the calculation by using equations, rectangular arrays, and/or area models.
description_plain: Your child can divide numbers up to four digits by one-digit numbers, find the quotient and remainder, and explain the steps using pictures or equations.

subject: Math
description: Multiply a whole number of up to four digits by a one-digit whole number, and multiply two two-digit numbers, using strategies based on place value and the properties of operations. Illustrate and explain the calculation by using equations, rectangular arrays, and/or area models.
description_plain: Your child can multiply a number up to four digits by a one-digit number and multiply two two-digit numbers, explaining their work with pictures or equations.

subject: Math
description: Fluently add and subtract multi-digit whole numbers using any algorithm. Verify the reasonableness of the results.
description_plain: Your child can quickly add and subtract large whole numbers using any method and check if the answers make sense.

subject: Math
description: Use place value understanding to round multi-digit whole numbers to any place using a variety of estimation methods; be able to describe, compare, and contrast solutions.
description_plain: Your child can round large numbers to any place using different methods and explain their answers.

subject: Math
description: Read and write multi-digit whole numbers using base-ten numerals, number names, and expanded form. Compare two multi-digit numbers based on the value of the digits in each place, using >, =, and < symbols to record the results of comparisons.
description_plain: Your child can read, write, and compare large numbers using digits, words, and expanded form, and use >, =, or < to show which is bigger.

subject: Math
description: Recognize that in a multi-digit whole number, a digit in one place represents ten times what it represents in the place to its right.
description_plain: Your child understands that in a number, a digit in one place is ten times bigger than the same digit in the place to its right.

subject: Math
description: Extend patterns that use addition, subtraction, multiplication, division or symbols, up to 10 terms, represented by models (function machines), tables, sequences, or in problem situations. (L)
description_plain: Your child can continue number or shape patterns using addition, subtraction, multiplication, or division up to 10 steps, shown in tables or models.

subject: Math
description: Generate a number, shape pattern, table, t-chart, or input/output function that follows a given rule. Identify apparent features of the pattern that were not explicit in the rule itself. Be able to express the pattern in algebraic terms.
description_plain: Your child can create a number or shape pattern that follows a rule, notice features of the pattern, and describe it using simple algebra.

subject: Math
description:
• Find all factor pairs for a whole number in the range 1–100.
  • Explain the correlation/differences between multiples and factors.
  • Determine whether a given whole number in the range 1–100 is a multiple of a given one-digit number.
  • Determine whether a given whole number in the range 1–100 is prime or composite.
description_plain: Your child can find all factor pairs of numbers from 1 to 100, explain how factors and multiples are related, decide if a number is a multiple of a one-digit number, and tell if a number is prime or composite.

subject: Math
description: Solve multi-step word problems posed with whole numbers and having whole-number answers using the four operations, including problems in which remainders must be interpreted. Represent these problems using equations with a letter standing for the unknown quantity. Assess the reasonableness of answers using mental computation and estimation strategies including rounding.
description_plain: Your child can solve multi-step word problems with whole numbers using addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division, including problems where remainders matter.

subject: Math
description: Multiply or divide to solve word problems involving multiplicative comparison (e.g., by using drawings and equations with a symbol for the unknown number to represent the problem or missing numbers in an array). Distinguish multiplicative comparison from additive comparison.
description_plain: Your child can solve word problems that compare amounts by multiplying or dividing and can tell the difference between multiplicative and additive comparisons.

subject: Math
description: Interpret a multiplication equation as a comparison e.g., interpret 35 = 5 × 7 as a statement that 35 is 5 groups of 7 and 7 groups of 5 (Commutative property). Represent verbal statements of multiplicative comparisons as multiplication equations.
description_plain: Your child understands multiplication equations as showing groups of equal size and can write multiplication equations from word problems about comparisons.

subject: Math
description: Look for and express regularity in repeated reasoning.
description_plain: Your child can notice and explain patterns or repeated steps when solving problems.

subject: Math
description: Look for and make use of structure.
description_plain: Your child can notice and use the way math problems are put together to help solve them.

subject: Math
description: Use appropriate tools strategically.
description_plain: Your child can choose and use the right tools, like calculators, rulers, or graphs, to help solve math problems.

subject: Math
description: Construct viable arguments and critique the reasoning of others.
description_plain: Your child can explain their math thinking and listen to and question the ideas of others.

subject: Math
description: Reason abstractly and quantitatively.
description_plain: Your child can think about math problems in different ways, using numbers and real-life examples.

subject: Math
description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them.
description_plain: Your child can understand what a math problem is asking and keep working until they find a solution.

subject: Math
description: Construct and interpret a two-way table summarizing data on two categorical variables collected from the same subjects and use relative frequencies to describe possible association between the two variables.
description_plain: Your child can make and read a two-way table that shows data about two categories from the same group, and use the data to describe if the two categories are related.

subject: Math
description: Use the equation of a linear model to solve problems in the context of bivariate measurement data, interpreting the slope and y-intercept.
description_plain: Your child can use a linear equation to solve problems with two related measurements and understand what the slope and intercept mean.

subject: Math
description: Explain why straight lines are widely used to model relationships between two quantitative variables. For scatter plots that suggest a linear association, informally fit a straight line, and informally assess the model fit by judging the closeness of the data points to the line.
description_plain: Your child understands why straight lines are used to show relationships between two numbers and can draw a line that fits data points on a scatter plot.

subject: Math
description: Construct and interpret scatter plots for bivariate measurement data to investigate patterns of association between two quantities. Describe patterns such as clustering, outliers, positive or negative association, linear association, and nonlinear association.
description_plain: Your child can make and read scatter plots to find patterns like groups, outliers, or whether two things increase or decrease together.

subject: Math
description: Given a verbal description between two quantities, sketch a graph. Conversely, given a graph, describe a possible real-world example.
description_plain: Your child can draw a graph from a description of how two things relate and explain a real-life example from a graph.

subject: Math
description: Construct a function to model a linear relationship between two quantities. Determine the rate of change and initial value of the function from a description of a relationship or from two (x, y) values, including reading these from a table or from a graph. Interpret the rate of change and initial value of a linear function in terms of the situation it models, and in terms of its graph or a table of values.
description_plain: Your child can create a function to show a straight-line relationship, find the rate of change and starting value from descriptions, tables, or graphs, and explain what they mean.

subject: Math
description: Interpret the equation y = mx + b as defining a linear function, whose graph is a straight line; give examples of functions that are not linear.
description_plain: Your child understands that y = mx + b is a rule for a straight-line function and can give examples of functions that are not straight lines.

subject: Math
description: Compare properties of two functions, each represented in a different way (algebraically, graphically, numerically in tables, or by verbal descriptions).
description_plain: Your child can compare two functions shown in different ways, like equations, graphs, tables, or words.

subject: Math
description: Understand that a function is a rule that assigns to each input (the domain) exactly one output (the range). The graph of a function is the set of ordered pairs consisting of an input and the corresponding output.
description_plain: Your child understands that a function is a rule that gives exactly one output for each input, and its graph shows all input-output pairs.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world and mathematical problems leading to two linear equations in two variables.
description_plain: Your child can solve real-life and math problems that involve two linear equations with two variables.

subject: Math
description: Solve systems of two linear equations in two variables and estimate solutions by graphing the equations. Simple cases may be done by inspection.
description_plain: Your child can solve systems of two linear equations by graphing or by simple inspection.

subject: Math
description: Show that the solution to a system of two linear equations in two variables is the intersection of the graphs of those equations because points of intersection satisfy both equations simultaneously.
description_plain: Your child understands that the solution to two linear equations is where their graphs cross because that point fits both equations.

subject: Math
description: Analyze and solve systems of linear equations.
description_plain: Your child can analyze and solve systems of linear equations.

subject: Math
description: Solve linear equations with rational coefficients, including equations whose solutions require expanding expressions using the distributive property and combining like terms.
description_plain: Your child can solve linear equations with fractions or decimals, including those that need expanding and combining like terms.

subject: Math
description: Give examples of linear equations in one variable with one solution, infinitely many solutions, or no solutions. Show which of these possibilities is the case by successively transforming the given equation into simpler forms, until an equivalent equation of the form x = a, a = a, or a = b results (where a and b are different numbers).
description_plain: Your child can show examples of linear equations that have one solution, many solutions, or no solution by simplifying the equations step-by-step.

subject: Math
description: Solve linear equations in one variable.
description_plain: Your child can solve linear equations with one variable.

subject: Math
description: Use similar triangles to explain why the slope m is the same between any two distinct points on a non-vertical line in the coordinate plane; derive the equation y = mx for a line through the origin and the equation y = mx + b for a line intercepting the vertical axis at b.
description_plain: Your child can explain why the slope is the same between any two points on a line and can write equations for lines through the origin or with a vertical intercept.

subject: Math
description: Graph linear equations such as y = mx + b, interpreting m as the slope or rate of change of the graph and b as the y-intercept or starting value. Compare two different proportional relationships represented in different ways.
description_plain: Your child can graph linear equations like y = mx + b, understand what the slope and intercept mean, and compare two proportional relationships shown in different ways.

subject: Math
description: Perform operations with numbers expressed in scientific notation, including problems where both standard notation and scientific notation are used. Use scientific notation and choose units of appropriate size for measurements of very large or very small quantities. Interpret scientific notation that has been generated by technology.
description_plain: Your child can work with very large or small numbers using scientific notation and choose the right units for measurements.

subject: Math
description: Use numbers expressed in the form of a single digit times an integer power of 10 to estimate very large or very small quantities, and to express how many times as much one is than the other.
description_plain: Your child can use numbers written as a digit times a power of 10 to estimate very big or small amounts and compare them.

subject: Math
description: Use square root and cube root symbols to represent solutions to equations of the form x² = p and x³ = p, where p is a positive rational number. Evaluate square roots of small perfect squares and cube roots of small perfect cubes. Know that √2 is irrational.
description_plain: Your child can use square roots and cube roots to solve equations and knows that some numbers like √2 cannot be written as simple fractions.

subject: Math
description: Apply the properties (product, quotient, power, zero, negative exponents, and rational exponents) of integer exponents to generate equivalent numerical expressions.
description_plain: Your child can use rules for exponents to rewrite and simplify expressions with powers.

subject: Math
description: Identify or write the prime factorization of a number using exponents. (L)
description_plain: Your child can find or write the prime factors of a number using exponents.

subject: Math
description: Order real numbers, using approximations of irrational numbers, locating them on a number line.
description_plain: Your child can order real numbers by estimating where irrational numbers go on a number line.

subject: Math
description: Classify real numbers as either rational (the ratio of two integers, a terminating decimal number, or a repeating decimal number) or irrational.
description_plain: Your child can tell whether a number is rational (can be written as a fraction or repeating decimal) or irrational.

subject: Math
description: Identify and apply the formulas for the volumes of cones, cylinders, and spheres and use them to solve real-world and mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child can use formulas to find the volumes of cones, cylinders, and spheres and solve related problems.

subject: Math
description: Apply the Pythagorean Theorem to find the distance between two points in a coordinate system.

subject: Math
description: Apply the Pythagorean Theorem to determine unknown side lengths in right triangles in real-world and mathematical problems in two and three dimensions.

subject: Math
description: Explain the Pythagorean Theorem and its converse.

subject: Math
description:
Justify using informal arguments to establish facts about
  • the angle sum of triangles (sum of the interior angles of a triangle is 180º),
  • measures of exterior angles of triangles,
  • angles created when parallel lines are cut be a transversal (e.g., alternate interior angles), and
  • angle-angle criterion for similarity of triangles.
description_plain: Your child can explain why the angles in triangles add up to 180°, understand exterior angles, angles formed by parallel lines and a transversal, and how to tell if triangles are similar by their angles.

subject: Math
description: Demonstrate understanding of similarity, by applying a sequence of translations, reflections, rotations, and dilations on two-dimensional figures. Describe a sequence that exhibits the similarity between them.

subject: Math

subject: Math

subject: Math
description:
For a function that models a relationship between two quantities,
  • interpret key features of graphs and tables in terms of the quantities, and
  • sketch graphs showing key features given a verbal description of the relationship.
Key features include: intercepts; intervals where the function is increasing, decreasing, positive, or negative; relative maximums and minimums; symmetries; end behavior; and periodicity.
description_plain: Your child can explain important parts of a function’s graph or table, like where it goes up or down, crosses axes, or repeats, and can draw graphs from descriptions.

description: Understand that a function from one set (called the domain) to another set (called the range) assigns to each element of the domain exactly one element of the range. If f is a function and x is an element of its domain, then f(x) denotes the output of f corresponding to the input x. The graph of f is the graph of the equation y = f(x).
description_plain: Your child understands that a function pairs each input with exactly one output, and that the graph of a function shows this relationship.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words and phrases acquired through conversations, reading and being read to, and responding to texts, including using adjectives and adverbs to describe (e.g., When other kids are happy that makes me happy).
description_plain: Your child uses new words and phrases learned from talking, reading, and listening, including describing words like adjectives and adverbs.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish shades of meaning among closely related verbs (e.g., toss, throw, hurl) and closely related adjectives (e.g., thin, slender, skinny, scrawny).
description_plain: Your child notices small differences in the meanings of similar verbs and adjectives.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify real-life connections between words and their use (e.g., describe foods that are spicy or juicy).
description_plain: Your child understands how words relate to real-life things, like describing foods as spicy or juicy.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of word relationships and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands how words are related and can notice small differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use glossaries and beginning picture dictionaries, both print and digital, to determine or clarify the meaning of words and phrases.
description_plain: Your child can use picture dictionaries and glossaries, in books or on a computer, to figure out what words and phrases mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of the meaning of individual words to predict the meaning of compound words (e.g., birdhouse, lighthouse, housefly; bookshelf, notebook, bookmark).
description_plain: Your child guesses the meaning of compound words by knowing the meanings of the smaller words inside them.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a known root word as a clue to the meaning of an unknown word with the same root (e.g., addition, additional).
description_plain: Your child uses the meaning of a root word to help understand new words with the same root.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of the new word formed when a known prefix is added to a known word (e.g., happy/unhappy, tell/retell).
description_plain: Your child figures out the meaning of new words by adding prefixes to words they know.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use sentence-level context as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child uses the sentence around a word to help understand what the word means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade 2 reading and content, choosing flexibly from an array of strategies.
description_plain: Your child uses different ways to figure out the meaning of new or tricky words when reading or learning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare formal and informal uses of English.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between formal and informal ways of speaking and writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.
description_plain: Your child uses what they know about language rules when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult reference materials, including beginning dictionaries, as needed to check and correct spellings.
description_plain: Your child knows how to use simple dictionaries or other resources to check and fix spelling mistakes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Generalize learned spelling patterns when writing words (e.g., cage → badge; boy → boil).
description_plain: Your child can use spelling patterns they have learned to write new words correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use an apostrophe to form contractions and frequently occurring possessives.
description_plain: Your child can use apostrophes to make contractions and show possession in words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commas in greetings and closing of letters.
description_plain: Your child will use commas correctly in the greetings and closings of letters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Capitalize holidays, product names, and geographic names.
description_plain: Your child capitalizes the names of holidays, products, and places on maps.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce, expand, and rearrange complete simple and compound sentences (e.g., The boy watched the movie; The little boy watched the movie; The action movie was watched by the little boy).
description_plain: Your child can write and change simple and compound sentences to add more detail or change the order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use adjectives and adverbs, and choose between them depending on what is to be modified.
description_plain: Your child uses describing words (adjectives) and words that tell how something is done (adverbs) correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use the past tense of frequently occurring irregular verbs (e.g., sat, hid, told).
description_plain: Your child knows how to use past tense forms of common irregular verbs like 'sat' or 'told'.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use reflexive pronouns (e.g., myself, ourselves).
description_plain: Your child uses reflexive pronouns like myself and ourselves when speaking or writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use frequently occurring irregular plural nouns (e.g., feet, children, teeth, mice, fish).
description_plain: Your child uses irregular plural nouns like 'feet' and 'children' properly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use collective nouns (e.g., group).
description_plain: Your child uses words that name groups of things, like 'group'.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and language rules when speaking and writing, building on what they learned before.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce complete sentences when appropriate to task and situation in order to provide requested detail or clarification. (See grade 2 Language standards 1 and 3 for specific expectations.)
description_plain: Your child speaks in complete sentences when needed to give details or explain something clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Create audio recordings of stories or poems; add drawings or other visual displays to stories or recounts of experiences when appropriate to clarify ideas, thoughts, and feelings.
description_plain: Your child can make audio recordings of stories or poems and add drawings or pictures to help explain their ideas and feelings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Tell a story or retell an experience with relevant facts and relevant, descriptive details, speaking audibly in coherent sentences.
description_plain: Your child can tell a story or share an experience clearly, using important facts and details, and speak so others can understand.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about what a speaker says in order to clarify comprehension, gather additional information, or deepen understanding of a topic or issue.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about what someone says to better understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Retell or describe key ideas or details from a text read aloud or information presented orally or through other media.
description_plain: Your child can retell or describe the main ideas or details from a story or information they hear or watch.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask for clarification and further explanation as needed about the topics and texts under discussions.
description_plain: Your child knows how to ask questions when they don’t understand something in a discussion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Build on others' talk in conversations by linking their comments to the remarks of others.
description_plain: Your child can join conversations by adding comments that connect to what others have said.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow agreed-upon rules for discussions (e.g., gaining the floor in respectful ways, listening to others with care, speaking one at a time about the topics and texts under discussion).
description_plain: Your child can follow rules for group talks, like waiting their turn, listening carefully, and speaking one at a time.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in collaborative conversations with diverse partners about grade 2 topics and texts with peers and adults in small and larger groups.
description_plain: Your child can take part in group conversations about grade 2 topics with classmates and adults.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recall information from experiences or gather information from provided sources to answer a question.
description_plain: Your child can remember information from experiences or find facts from sources to answer questions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in shared research and writing projects (e.g., read a number of books on a single topic to produce a report or visual or oral presentation; record data from science observations).
description_plain: Your child can work with others on research and writing projects, like reading books on one topic to make a report.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, use a variety of digital tools to produce and publish writing, including in collaboration with peers.
description_plain: With help, your child can use digital tools to create and share writing, and practice printing, cursive, or typing.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults and peers, focus on a topic and strengthen writing as needed (e.g., adding concrete and sensory details; elaborating on how the details chosen support the focus) by revising and editing.
description_plain: With help, your child can focus on a topic and improve their writing by adding details and fixing mistakes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use narrative writing to retell a well-elaborated event or short sequence of real or imagined events, include details to describe actions, thoughts, and feelings, use linking words to signal event order, and provide one or more concluding sentences that restate or emphasize a feeling or lesson learned.
description_plain: Your child can write a story about real or made-up events, including details about actions and feelings, use words that show the order of events, and finish with a sentence that shares a feeling or lesson.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts in which they introduce a topic, use facts and definitions to develop points, and provide a concluding statement or one or more concluding sentences that emphasize their most important point or focus.
description_plain: Your child can write informative texts that introduce a topic, give facts and definitions, and end with a conclusion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write opinion pieces in which they introduce the topic or book they are writing about, state an opinion, supply reasons that support the opinion, use linking words (e.g., because, and, also) to connect opinion and reasons, and provide one or more concluding sentences that restate or paraphrase their opinion.
description_plain: Your child can write opinion pieces that state a topic and opinion, give reasons, use linking words, and finish with a conclusion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, rereading as necessary.
description_plain: Your child uses the words around a tricky word to figure out or fix how to say or understand it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read on-level text orally with accuracy, appropriate rate, and expression on successive readings.
description_plain: Your child reads grade-level books out loud clearly, at a good speed, and with expression.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read on-level text with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child reads books at their grade level with understanding and purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read with sufficient accuracy and fluency to support comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can read accurately and smoothly enough to understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and read grade-appropriate irregularly spelled words.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and read common words that don’t follow usual spelling rules.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify words with inconsistent but common spelling-sound correspondences.
description_plain: Your child can spot words that have unusual but common ways of matching letters to sounds.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode words with common prefixes and suffixes.
description_plain: Your child can read words that have common beginnings or endings added.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode regularly spelled two-syllable words with long vowels.
description_plain: Your child can read two-syllable words with long vowel sounds that follow regular spelling patterns.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know spelling-sound correspondences for additional common vowel teams.
description_plain: Your child knows how to read vowel teams (two vowels that make one sound) in words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish long and short vowels when reading regularly spelled one-syllable words.
description_plain: Your child can hear and tell the difference between long and short vowel sounds in simple words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words.
description_plain: Your child uses phonics and word skills to sound out and read words at their grade level.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend a range of informational texts, including history/social studies, science, and technical texts within a complexity band appropriate to grade 2 (from upper grade 1 to grade 3), with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
description_plain: By the end of the year, your child can read and understand different kinds of informational texts about history, science, and other topics at their grade level.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the most important points presented by two texts or related topics (e.g., a book about polar bears and a book about black bears).
description_plain: Your child can find and explain the main similarities and differences between two texts or topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how reasons given support specific opinions the author states in a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how the reasons in a text support the author’s opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how specific images (e.g., a diagram showing how a machine works) contribute to and clarify a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how pictures or diagrams help explain the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the main purpose of a text, including what the author wants to answer, explain, or describe.
description_plain: Your child can identify the main purpose of a text, like what the author wants to explain or describe.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and use various text features (e.g., captions, bold print, headings, charts, bulleted or numbered lists, electronic menus, icons) to locate key facts or information in a text efficiently.
description_plain: Your child knows how to use parts of a text like captions, headings, and charts to quickly find important information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases in a text relevant to a grade 2 topic or subject area.
description_plain: Your child figures out the meaning of words and phrases in texts about grade 2 topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the connection between a series of historical events, scientific ideas or concepts, or steps in technical procedures in a text.
description_plain: Your child can describe how events, ideas, or steps in a text are connected.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the main topic of a multiparagraph text as well as the focus of specific paragraphs within the text.
description_plain: Your child can identify the main topic of a text and the focus of specific paragraphs.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer such questions as who, what, where, when, why, and how to demonstrate understanding of informational texts using key details from the text.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions like who, what, where, when, why, and how to show they understand important details in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend a range of literature from a variety of cultures, within a complexity band appropriate to grade 2 (from upper grade 1 to grade 3), with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
description_plain: By the end of the year, your child can read and understand stories and poems from different cultures at their grade level.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast two or more versions of the same story/text (e.g., Cinderella stories) by different authors or from different cultures.
description_plain: Your child can compare two or more versions of the same story written by different authors or from different cultures.

subject: Language Arts
description: (Not applicable to literature)
description_plain: This standard does not apply to literature for this grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use information gained from the illustrations and words in a print or digital text to demonstrate understanding of its characters, setting, or plot (e.g., problem-solution; chronology).
description_plain: Your child can use pictures and words in books or on screens to understand the characters, where the story happens, and what happens in the story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acknowledge differences in the points of view of characters, including by speaking in a different voice for each character when reading dialogue aloud.
description_plain: Your child understands that characters have different points of view and can show this by using different voices when reading their dialogue out loud.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the overall structure of a story, including describing how the beginning introduces the story (who, what, why, where), the middle describes the problem (how characters react or feel and what they do), and the ending concludes the action or tells how the problem was solved.
description_plain: Your child can describe how a story is put together, explaining how the beginning starts the story and the ending finishes it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify words and phrases that supply rhythm or sensory images and meaning in a story, poem, or song (e.g., regular beats, alliteration, rhymes, repeated lines) and describe how they make a reader feel or what a reader might see in his or her mind.
description_plain: Your child can find words and phrases in stories, poems, or songs that create rhythm or pictures in the mind and explain how they make a reader feel.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how characters in a story, play or poem respond to major events, problems, and challenges.
description_plain: Your child can describe how characters in stories or poems react to big events or problems.

subject: Language Arts
description: Retell stories, including fables and folktales from diverse cultures, and determine the author's purpose (e.g., teach a lesson, make you laugh, tell a scary story, describe an imaginary place), lesson or moral.
description_plain: Your child can retell stories from different cultures and explain the author’s purpose, like teaching a lesson or making you laugh.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer such questions as who, what, where, when, why, and how to demonstrate understanding of a literary text using key details from the text.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions like who, what, where, when, why, and how to show they understand important details in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately a range of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college- and career-readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge when encountering an unknown term important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child can learn and use a wide range of words for reading, writing, speaking, and listening, and can figure out new words on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands figurative language, how words relate to each other, and subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases by using context clues, analyzing meaningful word parts, and consulting general and specialized reference materials, as appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of unknown or tricky words by using clues in the sentence, looking at parts of the word, or checking dictionaries.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child understands how language works in different situations and can choose words and styles that fit what they want to say or understand better when reading or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and word usage when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and communicative tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can change how they speak depending on the situation and use formal English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make strategic use of digital media and visual displays of data to express information and enhance understanding of presentations.
description_plain: Your child can use digital tools and pictures to share information and help others understand their presentations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present information, findings, and supporting evidence such that listeners can follow the line of reasoning and that the organization, development, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can share information and evidence clearly so listeners can follow their ideas and understand the purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate a speaker's point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric.
description_plain: Your child can think about and judge a speaker’s opinions, reasons, and how they use evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child can combine and think about information from different types of media like videos, numbers, and spoken words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Prepare for and participate effectively in a range of conversations and collaborations with diverse partners, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly and persuasively.
description_plain: Your child can prepare for and take part in conversations with different people, building on others’ ideas and sharing their own clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child writes regularly over short and long periods for different reasons and audiences, including time for research and revising.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child can find and use evidence from stories or informational texts to support their thinking and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, assess the credibility and accuracy of each source, and integrate the information while avoiding plagiarism.
description_plain: Your child can gather information from many sources, check if the sources are trustworthy, and use the information without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short as well as more sustained research projects based on focused questions, demonstrating understanding of the subject under investigation.
description_plain: Your child can do short and longer research projects based on specific questions and show they understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child can use technology like the Internet to write, publish their work, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach.
description_plain: Your child can plan, revise, edit, and rewrite their writing to make it better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and organize their ideas in a way that fits the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use narrative writing to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, well-chosen details, and well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or imagined events using good details and clear order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine and convey complex ideas and information clearly and accurately through the effective selection, organization, and analysis of content.
description_plain: Your child can write informative texts that explain complex ideas clearly by choosing and organizing important information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims in an analysis of substantive topics or texts, using valid reasoning and relevant and sufficient evidence.
description_plain: Your child can write arguments that support their opinions with good reasons and evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend a range of complex literary and informational texts independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand difficult stories and informational texts on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can analyze how two or more texts on similar topics build knowledge or show different author approaches.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can find and explain the main argument and claims in a text, checking if the reasons and evidence make sense and are enough.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can combine and think about information from different types of media, like pictures, numbers, and words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child understands how the author's point of view or purpose changes what the text says and how it is written.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of the text (e.g., a section, chapter, scene, or stanza) relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can look at how sentences, paragraphs, and bigger parts of a text fit together and connect to the whole story or article.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text, including determining technical, connotative, and figurative meanings, and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a text, including special or hidden meanings, and explain how word choices affect the feeling or message.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, and ideas grow and connect throughout a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas or themes of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or themes in a text, explain how they develop, and summarize important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what the text says explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child reads carefully to understand what the text says and can make smart guesses, using examples from the text to support their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words and phrases acquired through conversations, reading and being read to, and responding to texts.
description_plain: Your child uses new words and phrases learned from talking, reading, and listening to stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish shades of meaning among verbs describing the same general action (e.g., walk, march, strut, prance) by acting out the meanings.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between similar action words like walk, march, or prance by acting them out.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify real-life connections between words and their use (e.g., note places at school that are colorful).
description_plain: Your child can connect words to real-life things, like noticing colorful places at school.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of frequently occurring verbs and adjectives by relating them to their opposites (antonyms).
description_plain: Your child can understand common action words and describing words by learning their opposites.

subject: Language Arts
description: Sort common objects into categories (e.g., shapes, foods) to gain a sense of the concepts the categories represent.
description_plain: Your child can group everyday objects, like shapes or foods, to understand what each group means.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, explore word relationships and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: With help from adults, your child explores how words are related and what different words mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the most frequently occurring inflections and affixes (e.g., -ed, -s, re-, un-, pre-, -ful, -less) as a clue to the meaning of an unknown word.
description_plain: Your child uses common word parts like -ed or un- to help figure out the meaning of new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify new meanings for familiar words and apply them accurately (e.g., knowing duck is a bird and learning the verb to duck).
description_plain: Your child understands that some words can have different meanings and can use them correctly, like knowing 'duck' is a bird and also a verb.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on kindergarten reading and content.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of new or tricky words based on what they read or hear in kindergarten.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell simple words phonetically, drawing on knowledge of sound-letter relationships.
description_plain: Your child spells simple words by sounding them out using what they know about letters and sounds.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write a letter or letters for most consonant and short-vowel sounds (phonemes).
description_plain: Your child writes letters that match most consonant and short vowel sounds they hear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and name end punctuation.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and name punctuation marks at the end of sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Capitalize the first word in a sentence, the first letter of the student's name, and the pronoun I.
description_plain: Your child can use capital letters at the start of sentences, for their name, and for the word I.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce and expand complete sentences in shared language activities.
description_plain: Your child can say and add to complete sentences during group language activities.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the most frequently occurring prepositions (e.g., to, from, in, out, on, off, for, of, by, with).
description_plain: Your child uses common prepositions like in, on, and with when speaking or writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Understand and use question words (interrogatives) (e.g., who, what, where, when, why, how).
description_plain: Your child understands and uses question words like who, what, where, when, why, and how.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form regular plural nouns orally by adding /s/ or /es/ (e.g., dog, dogs; wish, wishes).
description_plain: Your child can say plural nouns by adding sounds like /s/ or /es/ (for example, dog to dogs).

subject: Language Arts
description: Use frequently occurring nouns and verbs.
description_plain: Your child uses common nouns and action words when talking or writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Print many upper- and lowercase letters.
description_plain: Your child can print many uppercase and lowercase letters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and language rules when speaking or writing and builds on skills learned before.

subject: Language Arts
description: Speak audibly and express thoughts, feelings, and ideas clearly in complete sentences when appropriate to task and situation.
description_plain: Your child can speak clearly and loudly enough to share their thoughts, feelings, and ideas in full sentences when it fits the situation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Add drawings or other visual displays to descriptions as desired to provide additional details.
description_plain: Your child can add drawings or pictures to their descriptions to give more details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe familiar people, places, things, and events, and with prompting and support, provide additional related details.
description_plain: Your child can describe people, places, things, and events they know, and with help, add more related details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions in order to seek help, get information, or clarify something that is not understood.
description_plain: Your child asks and answers questions to get help, learn new information, or understand better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Confirm understanding of a text read aloud or information presented orally or through other media by asking and answering questions about key details and requesting clarification if something is not understood.
description_plain: Your child shows they understand stories or information by asking and answering questions and asking for help if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Continue a conversation through multiple exchanges.
description_plain: Your child can keep a conversation going by taking turns talking several times.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow agreed-upon rules for discussions (e.g., listening to others and taking turns speaking about the topics and texts under discussion).
description_plain: Your child follows rules for talking with others, like listening and taking turns.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in collaborative conversations with diverse partners about kindergarten topics and texts with peers and adults in small and larger groups.
description_plain: Your child joins in conversations with different people about kindergarten topics and stories in small or large groups.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, recall information from experiences or gather information from provided sources to answer a question.
description_plain: With help, your child can remember or find information from experiences or sources to answer questions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in shared research and writing projects (e.g., explore a number of books by a favorite author and combine or summarize information/facts learned or express opinions about them).
description_plain: Your child takes part in group research and writing projects, like reading books by a favorite author and sharing opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, explore a variety of digital tools to produce and publish writing, including in collaboration with peers.
description_plain: Your child can use digital tools with help from adults to create and share writing, sometimes working with friends, and can write by printing letters, writing in cursive, or typing.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, respond to questions and suggestions from peers and add details to strengthen writing as needed.
description_plain: Your child can listen to questions and ideas from friends and add more details to their writing with adult help.

subject: Language Arts
