description: Use a combination of drawing, dictating, and writing to narrate a real or imagined event or several loosely linked events, tell about the events in the order in which they occurred, and provide an ending (how the problem was solved) or a reaction (e.g., a feeling) to what happened.
description_plain: Your child can tell a story about real or made-up events by drawing, talking, or writing, telling what happened in order and how it ended or how they felt.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a combination of drawing, dictating, and writing to compose informative/explanatory texts in which they name what they are writing about and supply some information about the topic.
description_plain: Your child can use drawing, speaking, and writing to explain a topic by naming it and giving some information about it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a combination of drawing, dictating, and writing to state an opinion or a preference about a topic or part of a book (e.g., I like dogs better than cats because…; My favorite part of the story is when…; I think it was funny when…).
description_plain: Your child can share their opinion or favorite part about a topic or book by drawing, talking, or writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read emergent-reader texts with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child can read simple books carefully and understand what they mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish between similarly spelled words by identifying the sounds of the letters that differ.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between words that look alike by listening to the different sounds in the letters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read common high-frequency words by sight. (e.g., the, of, to, you, she, my, is, are, do, does).
description_plain: Your child can recognize and read common words by sight, like 'the,' 'you,' and 'is.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Associate the long and short sounds with the common spellings (graphemes) for the five major vowels.
description_plain: Your child can connect the long and short vowel sounds to the common letters that make those sounds.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate basic knowledge of one-to-one letter-sound correspondences by producing the primary sound or many of the most frequent sounds for each consonant.
description_plain: Your child can say the main sounds for most letters, especially consonants.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words.
description_plain: Your child uses phonics and word skills to sound out and read words at their grade level.

subject: Language Arts
description: Add or substitute individual sounds (phonemes) in simple, one-syllable words to make new words.
description_plain: Your child can change or add sounds in simple one-syllable words to make new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Isolate and pronounce the initial, medial vowel, and final sounds (phonemes) in three-phoneme (consonant-vowel-consonant, or CVC) words. (This does not include CVCs ending with /l/, /r/, or /x/.)
description_plain: Your child can say the beginning, middle, and ending sounds in simple three-letter words like 'cat' or 'dog.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Blend and segment onsets and rimes of single-syllable spoken words.
description_plain: Your child can put together and break apart parts of words that rhyme and sound alike.

subject: Language Arts
description: Count, pronounce, blend, and segment syllables in spoken words.
description_plain: Your child can count and say the parts (syllables) in spoken words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and produce rhyming words.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and say words that rhyme.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of spoken words, syllables, and sounds (phonemes).
description_plain: Your child understands how words are made up of sounds and parts like syllables.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and name all upper- and lowercase letters of the alphabet.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and name all uppercase and lowercase letters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Understand that words are separated by spaces in print.
description_plain: Your child understands that words are separated by spaces when reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize that spoken words are represented in written language by specific sequences of letters.
description_plain: Your child understands that spoken words match specific letters in writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow words from left to right, top to bottom, and page-by-page.
description_plain: Your child can follow words in a book from left to right, top to bottom, and page by page.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of the organization and basic features of print.
description_plain: Your child understands how print is organized and what it looks like.

subject: Language Arts
description: Actively engage in shared reading activities using a range of topics and texts with purpose and understanding, with scaffolding as needed.
description_plain: Your child can join in reading activities with different topics and books, understanding and focusing on the purpose, with help if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, identify basic similarities in and differences between information presented in two texts on the same topic (e.g., compare two photos or diagrams, compare two animal babies).
description_plain: With help, your child can find simple similarities and differences between two texts about the same topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, identify the opinions an author states in a text.
description_plain: With help, your child can find the opinions the author shares in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, describe the relationship between illustrations and the text in which they appear (e.g., what person, place, thing, or idea in the text an illustration depicts).
description_plain: With help, your child can explain how pictures in a book show people, places, or ideas from the words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Name the author and illustrator of a text and describe the role of each in presenting the ideas or information in a text.
description_plain: Your child can name who wrote and who drew the pictures in a book and explain how each helped share the ideas or information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the front cover, back cover, and title page of a book.
description_plain: Your child can find the front cover, back cover, and title page of a book.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, ask and answer questions about unknown words in a text.
description_plain: With help, your child can ask and answer questions about words they don’t know in a story or text.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, describe the connection between two individuals, events, ideas, or pieces of information in a text.
description_plain: With help, your child can explain how two people, events, or ideas in a book are connected.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, identify the main topic and retell key details of a text.
description_plain: With help, your child can tell the main topic of a book and share important details about it.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, elicit background/prior knowledge and experience in order to ask and answer questions about an informational text using key details from the text.
description_plain: With help, your child can use what they already know to ask and answer questions about an informational text using important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Actively engage in shared reading activities using literature from a variety of cultures with purpose and understanding, and scaffolding as needed.
description_plain: Your child can join in reading stories from different cultures, understanding and focusing on the story, with help if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, compare and contrast the adventures and experiences of characters in familiar stories.
description_plain: With help, your child can tell how characters in stories have similar or different adventures and experiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: (Not applicable to literature)
description_plain: This standard does not apply to literature at this grade level.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, describe the relationship between illustrations and the story in which they appear (e.g., what moment in a story an illustration depicts) or use illustrations to tell or retell a story.
description_plain: With help, your child can explain how pictures in a story show a moment or part of the story.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, name the author and illustrator of a story and describe the role of each in telling the story.
description_plain: With help, your child can name the author and illustrator of a story and explain how each helped tell the story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify common types of texts (e.g., picture books, stories, poems, songs).
description_plain: Your child can recognize common kinds of texts like picture books, stories, poems, and songs.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about unknown words in a text.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about words they don’t know in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, identify characters, settings, major events, and problem-solution in a story, song, or poem.
description_plain: With help, your child can identify characters, places, main events, and problems and solutions in stories, songs, or poems.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, retell familiar stories, using key details.
description_plain: With help, your child can retell familiar stories using important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, ask and answer questions about a literary text using key details from the text.
description_plain: With help, your child can ask and answer questions about a story using important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately a range of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college- and career-readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge when encountering an unknown term important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child can learn and use a wide range of words for reading, writing, speaking, and listening, and can figure out new words on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands figurative language, how words relate to each other, and subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases by using context clues, analyzing meaningful word parts, and consulting general and specialized reference materials, as appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of unknown or tricky words by using clues in the sentence, looking at parts of the word, or checking dictionaries.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child understands how language works in different situations and can choose words and styles that fit what they want to say or understand better when reading or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and word usage when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and communicative tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can change how they speak depending on the situation and use formal English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make strategic use of digital media and visual displays of data to express information and enhance understanding of presentations.
description_plain: Your child can use digital tools and pictures to share information and help others understand their presentations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present information, findings, and supporting evidence such that listeners can follow the line of reasoning and that the organization, development, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can share information and evidence clearly so listeners can follow their ideas and understand the purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate a speaker's point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric.
description_plain: Your child can think about and judge a speaker’s opinions, reasons, and how they use evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child can combine and think about information from different types of media like videos, numbers, and spoken words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Prepare for and participate effectively in a range of conversations and collaborations with diverse partners, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly and persuasively.
description_plain: Your child can prepare for and take part in conversations with different people, building on others’ ideas and sharing their own clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child writes regularly over short and long periods for different reasons and audiences, including time for research and revising.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child can find and use evidence from stories or informational texts to support their thinking and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, assess the credibility and accuracy of each source, and integrate the information while avoiding plagiarism.
description_plain: Your child can gather information from many sources, check if the sources are trustworthy, and use the information without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short as well as more sustained research projects based on focused questions, demonstrating understanding of the subject under investigation.
description_plain: Your child can do short and longer research projects based on specific questions and show they understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child can use technology like the Internet to write, publish their work, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach.
description_plain: Your child can plan, revise, edit, and rewrite their writing to make it better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and organize their ideas in a way that fits the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use narrative writing to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, well-chosen details, and well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or imagined events using good details and clear order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine and convey complex ideas and information clearly and accurately through the effective selection, organization, and analysis of content.
description_plain: Your child can write informative texts that explain complex ideas clearly by choosing and organizing important information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims in an analysis of substantive topics or texts, using valid reasoning and relevant and sufficient evidence.
description_plain: Your child can write arguments that support their opinions with good reasons and evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend a range of complex literary and informational texts independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand difficult stories and informational texts on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can analyze how two or more texts on similar topics build knowledge or show different author approaches.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can find and explain the main argument and claims in a text, checking if the reasons and evidence make sense and are enough.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can combine and think about information from different types of media, like pictures, numbers, and words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child understands how the author's point of view or purpose changes what the text says and how it is written.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of the text (e.g., a section, chapter, scene, or stanza) relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can look at how sentences, paragraphs, and bigger parts of a text fit together and connect to the whole story or article.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text, including determining technical, connotative, and figurative meanings, and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a text, including special or hidden meanings, and explain how word choices affect the feeling or message.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, and ideas grow and connect throughout a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas or themes of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or themes in a text, explain how they develop, and summarize important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what the text says explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child reads carefully to understand what the text says and can make smart guesses, using examples from the text to support their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and accurately use grade-appropriate conversational, general academic, and domain-specific words and phrases, including those that signal spatial and temporal relationships (e.g., After dinner that night we went looking for them).
description_plain: Your child can learn and use new words that help describe where and when things happen in conversations and schoolwork.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish shades of meaning among related words (e.g., words that describe states of mind or degrees of certainty, such as knew, believed ).
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between similar words that show different feelings or levels of certainty, like 'knew' and 'believed.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify real-life connections between words and their use (e.g., describe people who are friendly or helpful).
description_plain: Your child can connect words to real-life situations, like describing friendly or helpful people.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish the literal and nonliteral meanings of words and phrases in context (e.g., take steps).
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between the literal meaning and the figurative or hidden meaning of words and phrases.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of word relationships and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands how words relate to each other and notices small differences in their meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use glossaries or beginning dictionaries, both print and digital, to determine or clarify the precise meaning of key words and phrases.
description_plain: Your child uses glossaries or simple dictionaries, in books or online, to find out what important words mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a known root word as a clue to the meaning of an unknown word with the same root (e.g., company, companion).
description_plain: Your child uses the meaning of a root word they know to help understand new words with the same root.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of a new word formed when a known affix is added to a known word (e.g., agreeable/disagreeable, comfortable/uncomfortable, care/careless, heat/preheat).
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of new words by looking at the parts added to familiar words, like 'disagreeable' or 'preheat.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a sentence-level context as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can use the sentence around a word to help understand what that word means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning word and phrases based on grade 3 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of unknown or tricky words using different strategies while reading or learning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and observe differences between the conventions of spoken and written standard English.
description_plain: Your child can notice and understand the differences between how people speak and how they write in standard English.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose words and phrases for effect.
description_plain: Your child picks words and phrases to make their writing more interesting or clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.
description_plain: Your child uses what they know about language rules when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult reference materials, including beginning dictionaries, as needed to check and correct spelling.
description_plain: Your child uses dictionaries or other resources to check and fix spelling when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use spelling patterns and generalizations (e.g., word families, position-based spellings, syllable patterns, ending rules, meaningful word parts) in writing words.
description_plain: Your child uses spelling rules and patterns, like word families and syllables, to write words correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use conventional spelling for high-frequency and other studied words and for adding suffixes to base words (e.g., sitting, smiled, cries, happiness)
description_plain: Your child spells common words and adds endings like -ing or -ed correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commas and quotation marks in dialogue.
description_plain: Your child can use commas and quotation marks correctly when writing conversations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Capitalize appropriate words in titles.
description_plain: Your child will capitalize the right words in titles.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce simple, compound, and complex sentences.
description_plain: Your child can write simple, compound, and complex sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use coordinating and subordinating conjunctions.
description_plain: Your child uses words like 'and,' 'but,' and 'because' to connect ideas in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use comparative and superlative adjectives and adverbs, and choose between them depending on what is to be modified.
description_plain: Your child can use and choose the correct forms of describing words and words that tell how things happen, like 'bigger' or 'most quickly.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Ensure subject-verb and pronoun-antecedent agreement
description_plain: Your child can make sure subjects and verbs, and pronouns and the words they replace, agree in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use the simple (e.g., I walked; I walk; I will walk) verb tenses.
description_plain: Your child uses different verb tenses like past, present, and future correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use regular and irregular verbs.
description_plain: Your child knows how to use regular and irregular verbs properly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use abstract nouns (e.g., childhood).
description_plain: Your child uses nouns that name ideas or feelings, like 'childhood.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use regular and irregular plural nouns.
description_plain: Your child can make nouns plural, including those that don’t follow usual rules.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use nouns, pronouns, verbs, adjectives, and adverbs appropriate to function and purpose in order to apply the conventions of English.
description_plain: Your child can use nouns, pronouns, verbs, adjectives, and adverbs correctly to follow English rules.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and language rules when writing or speaking and builds on what they learned before.

subject: Language Arts
description: Speak in complete sentences when appropriate to task and situation in order to provide requested detail or clarification. (See grade 3 Language standards 1 and 3 for specific expectations.)
description_plain: Your child speaks in full sentences when needed to explain or give more details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Create audio recordings of stories or poems that demonstrate fluid reading at an understandable pace; use techniques that engage the listener (e.g., inflection, different voices); and add visual displays when appropriate to emphasize or enhance certain facts or details.
description_plain: Your child can make audio recordings of stories or poems that sound smooth and clear, use different voices or feelings to keep listeners interested, and add pictures when helpful.

subject: Language Arts
description: Report on a topic or text, tell a story, or recount an experience with appropriate facts and relevant, descriptive details, speaking clearly at an understandable pace.
description_plain: Your child tells about a topic, story, or experience using facts and details, speaking clearly and at a good speed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about information from a speaker, offering appropriate elaboration or explanations and detail.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about what a speaker says, giving more details or explanations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the main ideas and supporting details of a text read aloud or information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child finds the main ideas and important details when listening to stories or information in different formats.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain their own ideas and understanding in light of the discussion.
description_plain: Your child explains their own ideas and what they understand during discussions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask questions to check understanding of information presented, stay on topic, and link their comments to the remarks of others.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions to understand what others say, stay focused on the topic, and connect their ideas to what others have shared.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow agreed-upon rules for discussions (e.g., gaining the floor in respectful ways, listening to others with care, speaking one at a time about the topics and texts under discussion).
description_plain: Your child will follow simple rules during discussions, like waiting their turn to speak, listening carefully, and talking one at a time about the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: After learning a protocol (e.g., Socratic method), come to discussions prepared, having read or studied required material; explicitly draw on that preparation and other information known about the topic to explore ideas under discussion.
description_plain: Your child comes to group talks ready by reading or studying ahead and uses what they learned to share ideas during discussions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher-led) with diverse partners on grade 3 topics and texts, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly.
description_plain: Your child can join different kinds of group talks, listen to others, build on their ideas, and clearly share their own thoughts about grade 3 topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child writes regularly, sometimes over several days and sometimes in one sitting, for different subjects and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recall information from experiences or gather information from print and digital sources; take brief notes on sources and sort evidence into provided categories.
description_plain: Your child remembers information from experiences or finds facts from books and websites, takes short notes, and organizes the information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects that build knowledge about a topic.
description_plain: Your child does short research projects to learn more about a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, use technology to produce and publish writing (using keyboarding skills) as well as to interact and collaborate with others and to locate information about topics.
description_plain: With help from adults, your child uses computers or tablets to write and share their work and to work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, and editing. (Editing for conventions should demonstrate command of Language standards 1–3 up to and including grade 3.)
description_plain: With help from adults and friends, your child plans, revises, and edits their writing to make it better.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, produce writing in which the development and organization are appropriate to task and purpose. (Grade-specific expectations for writing types are defined in standards 1–3 above.)
description_plain: With adult support, your child writes pieces that are organized and fit the purpose of the task.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a sense of closure (e.g., how a problem was solved or how the event ended).
description_plain: Your child can finish a story by explaining how the problem was solved or how things ended.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use transitional words and phrases to signal event sequences (e.g., later, soon after).
description_plain: Your child can use words like 'later' or 'soon after' to show the order of events in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use narrative techniques, such as dialogue, description and elaboration, and concrete and sensory details to describe actions, thoughts, and feelings and to develop experiences and events showing the response of characters to situations or problems.
description_plain: Your child can use dialogue, descriptions, and details that show actions, thoughts, and feelings to make stories more interesting and show how characters react.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish a context or situation and introduce a narrator and/or characters; organize an event sequence that unfolds naturally.
description_plain: Your child can start a story by setting the scene and introducing characters or a narrator, and tell events in a natural order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use narrative writing to develop real or imagined characters, experiences, or events using effective narrative techniques (dialogue, description, elaboration, problem-solution, figurative language), and clear event sequences (chronology).
description_plain: Your child can write stories with real or made-up characters and events, using dialogue, descriptions, and clear order of events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that paraphrases the focus of the text.
description_plain: Your child can write an ending that restates the main idea of their writing in their own words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use linking words and phrases (e.g., also, another, and, more, but) to connect ideas within categories of information.
description_plain: Your child uses words like also, and, and but to connect ideas in writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with facts, definitions, details, and explanations that support the focus.
description_plain: Your child can add facts, definitions, and details to explain their topic clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic and group related information together; include labeled or captioned visuals when useful to aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can introduce a topic and group related information together, adding pictures or labels when helpful.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine a topic and convey ideas and information clearly.
description_plain: Your child writes to explain a topic clearly and share information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that reinforces or restates the opinion.
description_plain: Your child can write a conclusion that restates their opinion clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Link opinion and reasons using words and phrases (e.g., because, therefore, since, for example).
description_plain: Your child can connect their opinion and reasons using words like because, since, or for example.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide reasons that support the opinion.
description_plain: Your child gives reasons to support their opinion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce the topic or text they are writing about, state an opinion, and create an organizational structure that lists fact- or text-based reasons.
description_plain: Your child introduces a topic, states an opinion, and organizes reasons to support it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write opinion pieces on topics or texts, supporting a point of view with fact- or text-based reasons (e.g., I like large dogs better than small dogs because they can pull a sled and run for a longer time than small dogs can).
description_plain: Your child can write about their opinion on a topic and give reasons based on facts or texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, rereading as necessary.
description_plain: Your child uses clues from the text to figure out or fix words they don’t know, rereading if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read on-level prose and poetry orally with accuracy, appropriate rate, and expression on successive readings.
description_plain: Your child can read stories and poems out loud accurately, at a good speed, and with expression.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read on-level text with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child reads grade-level text with understanding and purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read with sufficient accuracy and fluency to support comprehension.
description_plain: Your child reads accurately and smoothly enough to understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-appropriate irregularly spelled words.
description_plain: Your child reads grade-appropriate words that are spelled in unusual ways.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode words with common Latin suffixes.
description_plain: Your child reads words with common Latin endings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify and know the meaning of the most common prefixes and derivational suffixes.
description_plain: Your child knows common beginnings and endings of words and what they mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words.
description_plain: Your child uses phonics and word parts to read new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend a range of informational texts, including history/social studies, science, and technical texts within a complexity band appropriate to grade 3 (from upper grade 2 to grade 4), with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand different informational texts about history, science, and technology at a third-grade level.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the most important points and key details presented in two texts on the same topic or related topics.
description_plain: Your child compares and contrasts the main points and details in two texts about the same topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the logical connection between particular sentences and paragraphs in a text (e.g., comparison, cause/effect, first/second/third in a sequence).
description_plain: Your child explains how sentences and paragraphs in a text are connected, like showing cause and effect or order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use information gained from illustrations (e.g., maps, photographs), and the words in a text to demonstrate understanding of the text (e.g., where, when, why, and how key events occur).
description_plain: Your child can use pictures and words in a text to explain where, when, why, and how important events happen.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine author's purpose; distinguish own point of view from that of the author of a text.
description_plain: Your child can figure out why the author wrote a text and tell how their own opinion is different from the author's.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use text features and search tools (e.g., table of contents, index, key words, sidebars, hyperlinks) to locate information relevant to a given topic efficiently.
description_plain: Your child can use parts of a book like the table of contents or index to quickly find information on a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases in a text relevant to a grade 3 topic or subject area.
description_plain: Your child figures out the meaning of important words related to grade 3 topics in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the relationship or connection among a series of historical events, scientific ideas or concepts, or steps in technical procedures in a text, using language that pertains to time, sequence, and cause/effect.
description_plain: Your child can explain how events or ideas are connected by time, order, or cause and effect in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the main idea of a text and locate details that support the main idea; paraphrase or summarize main ideas or events in a multi-paragraph text, including correct sequence and details that support the main idea.
description_plain: Your child can find the main idea of a text and the details that support it, then retell the main points in order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions to demonstrate understanding of a text, (e.g., explaining what the texts says explicitly, making basic inferences and predictions), referring explicitly to the text as the basis for the answers.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about a text, using the text to explain their answers.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend a range of literature from a variety of cultures, within a complexity band appropriate to grade 3 (from upper grade 2 to grade 4), with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand stories from different cultures at a third-grade level.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the themes, settings, and plots of stories written by the same author about the same or similar characters (e.g., in books from a series).
description_plain: Your child can compare and tell how themes, settings, and plots are alike or different in stories by the same author about similar characters.

subject: Language Arts
description: (Not applicable to literature)
description_plain: This standard does not apply to literature.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how specific aspects of text's illustrations contribute to what is conveyed by the words in a story (e.g., create mood, emphasize aspects of a character or setting).
description_plain: Your child can explain how pictures in a story help show the mood or details about characters and settings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish their own point of view from that of the narrator or those of the characters.
description_plain: Your child understands how their own opinion is different from the narrator’s or characters’ points of view.

subject: Language Arts
description: Refer to parts of stories, dramas, and poems when writing or speaking about a text, using terms such as chapter, scene, and stanza; describe how each successive part builds on earlier sections.
description_plain: Your child can talk or write about parts of stories, plays, or poems using words like chapter, scene, or stanza, and explain how each part adds to the story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, distinguishing literal meanings from use of figurative language (e.g., exaggeration in tall tales).
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a story, including when they are used in a special or imaginative way.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe characters in a story (e.g., their traits, motivations, or feelings) and explain how their actions contribute to the sequence of events (e.g., creating or solving a problem).
description_plain: Your child can describe characters’ traits, feelings, and reasons for acting, and explain how their actions move the story forward.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the author's purpose, message, lesson, or moral and explain how it is conveyed through key details in the text; summarize stories in correct sequence, including fables and folktales from diverse cultures.
description_plain: Your child can tell the author's message or lesson in a story and explain it using important details, including fables and folktales.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions to demonstrate understanding of a text (e.g., making basic inferences and predictions), referring explicitly to details from the text as the basis for the answers.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about a story, using details from the text to support their answers.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately a range of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college- and career-readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge when encountering an unknown term important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child can learn and use a wide range of words for reading, writing, speaking, and listening, and can figure out new words on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands figurative language, how words relate to each other, and subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases by using context clues, analyzing meaningful word parts, and consulting general and specialized reference materials, as appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of unknown or tricky words by using clues in the sentence, looking at parts of the word, or checking dictionaries.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child understands how language works in different situations and can choose words and styles that fit what they want to say or understand better when reading or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and word usage when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and communicative tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can change how they speak depending on the situation and use formal English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make strategic use of digital media and visual displays of data to express information and enhance understanding of presentations.
description_plain: Your child can use digital tools and pictures to share information and help others understand their presentations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present information, findings, and supporting evidence such that listeners can follow the line of reasoning and that the organization, development, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can share information and evidence clearly so listeners can follow their ideas and understand the purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate a speaker's point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric.
description_plain: Your child can think about and judge a speaker’s opinions, reasons, and how they use evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child can combine and think about information from different types of media like videos, numbers, and spoken words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Prepare for and participate effectively in a range of conversations and collaborations with diverse partners, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly and persuasively.
description_plain: Your child can prepare for and take part in conversations with different people, building on others’ ideas and sharing their own clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child writes regularly over short and long periods for different reasons and audiences, including time for research and revising.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child can find and use evidence from stories or informational texts to support their thinking and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, assess the credibility and accuracy of each source, and integrate the information while avoiding plagiarism.
description_plain: Your child can gather information from many sources, check if the sources are trustworthy, and use the information without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short as well as more sustained research projects based on focused questions, demonstrating understanding of the subject under investigation.
description_plain: Your child can do short and longer research projects based on specific questions and show they understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child can use technology like the Internet to write, publish their work, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach.
description_plain: Your child can plan, revise, edit, and rewrite their writing to make it better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and organize their ideas in a way that fits the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use narrative writing to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, well-chosen details, and well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or imagined events using good details and clear order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine and convey complex ideas and information clearly and accurately through the effective selection, organization, and analysis of content.
description_plain: Your child can write informative texts that explain complex ideas clearly by choosing and organizing important information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims in an analysis of substantive topics or texts, using valid reasoning and relevant and sufficient evidence.
description_plain: Your child can write arguments that support their opinions with good reasons and evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend a range of complex literary and informational texts independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand difficult stories and informational texts on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can analyze how two or more texts on similar topics build knowledge or show different author approaches.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can find and explain the main argument and claims in a text, checking if the reasons and evidence make sense and are enough.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can combine and think about information from different types of media, like pictures, numbers, and words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child understands how the author's point of view or purpose changes what the text says and how it is written.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of the text (e.g., a section, chapter, scene, or stanza) relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can look at how sentences, paragraphs, and bigger parts of a text fit together and connect to the whole story or article.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text, including determining technical, connotative, and figurative meanings, and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a text, including special or hidden meanings, and explain how word choices affect the feeling or message.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, and ideas grow and connect throughout a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas or themes of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or themes in a text, explain how they develop, and summarize important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what the text says explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child reads carefully to understand what the text says and can make smart guesses, using examples from the text to support their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for reflection and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child can write regularly over short and long times for different school subjects, purposes, and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child uses evidence from informational texts to support their analysis, reflection, and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, using search terms effectively; assess the credibility and accuracy of each source; and quote or paraphrase the data and conclusions of others while avoiding plagiarism and following a standard format for citation.
description_plain: Your child can find useful information from books and online, check if sources are trustworthy, and use others' ideas correctly without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects to answer a question (including a self-generated question), drawing on several sources and generating additional related, focused questions that allow for multiple avenues of exploration.
description_plain: Your child can do short research projects to answer questions, using several sources and asking more focused questions to explore.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and present the relationships between information and ideas clearly and efficiently.
description_plain: Your child can use technology like the Internet to write and share their work clearly and effectively.

subject: Language Arts
description: With some guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach, focusing on how well purpose and audience have been addressed.
description_plain: With some help, your child can improve their writing by planning, revising, editing, or trying new ways to better reach their audience and purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and organized in a way that fits the task, purpose, and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Not applicable as a separate requirement.
description_plain: This standard is not a separate requirement.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the information or explanation presented.
description_plain: Your child can write a conclusion that clearly follows from and supports the information or explanation they gave.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a formal style and objective tone.
description_plain: Your child can write in a formal style and keep an objective tone.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to inform about or explain the topic.
description_plain: Your child can use clear and specific language and special vocabulary to explain a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use appropriate and varied transitions to create cohesion and clarify the relationships among ideas and concepts.
description_plain: Your child can use different transitions to connect ideas and make their writing clear and easy to follow.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with relevant facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations, or other information and examples.
description_plain: Your child can add facts, definitions, details, and quotes to explain a topic clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic clearly, previewing what is to follow; organize ideas, concepts, and information into broader categories as appropriate to achieving purpose; include formatting (e.g., headings) graphics (e.g., charts, tables), and multimedia when useful to aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can clearly introduce a topic, organize ideas into groups, and use headings, charts, or multimedia to help readers understand.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts, including the narration of historical events, scientific procedures/experiments, or technical processes.
description_plain: Your child can write informative texts that explain history events, science experiments, or technical steps.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the argument presented.
description_plain: Your child can write a conclusion that supports and follows from the argument they made.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a formal style.
description_plain: Your child can write in a formal style.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words, phrases, and clauses to create cohesion and clarify the relationships among claim(s), counterclaims, reasons, and evidence.
description_plain: Your child can use words and phrases to connect claims, opposing claims, reasons, and evidence clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Support claim(s) with logical reasoning and relevant, accurate data and evidence that demonstrate an understanding of the topic or text, using credible sources.
description_plain: Your child can support their claims with logical reasons and accurate facts from trustworthy sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce claim(s) abut a topic or issue, acknowledge and distinguish the claim(s) from alternate or opposing claims, and organize the reasons and evidence logically.
description_plain: Your child can introduce their opinion on a topic, recognize other opinions, and organize their reasons and evidence clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments focused on discipline-specific content.
description_plain: Your child can write arguments about topics in specific school subjects.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of grade 8, read and comprehend science/technical texts in the grades 6-8 text complexity band independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand science and technical texts for grades 6 to 8 on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the information gained from experiments, simulations, video, or multimedia sources with that gained from reading a text on the same topic.
description_plain: Your child can compare information from experiments, videos, or simulations with what they read about the same topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish among facts, reasoned judgment based on research findings, and speculation in a text.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between facts, opinions based on research, and guesses in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate quantitative or technical information expressed in words in a text with a version of that information expressed visually (e.g., in a flowchart, diagram, model, graph, or table).
description_plain: Your child can combine information given in words with pictures, charts, or graphs to understand a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analysis the author's purpose in providing an explanation, describing a procedure, or discussing an experiment in a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain why an author gives instructions, describes a process, or talks about an experiment.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure an author uses to organize a text, including how the major sections contribute to the whole and to an understanding of the topic.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author organizes a text and how its parts help explain the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of symbols, key terms, and other domain-specific words and phrases as they are used in a specific scientific or technical context relevant to grades 6-8 texts and topics.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what symbols and special words mean when reading science or technical texts for grades 6–8.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow precisely a multistep procedure when carrying out experiments, taking measurements, or performing technical tasks.
description_plain: Your child can carefully follow multi-step instructions when doing experiments, measuring, or technical tasks.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the central ideas or conclusions of a text; provide an accurate summary of the text distinct from prior knowledge or opinions.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or conclusions in a text and give a clear summary without mixing in their own opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite specific textual evidence to support analysis of science and technical texts.
description_plain: Your child can point to exact parts of a science or technical text to support their understanding and analysis.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of grade 8, read and comprehend history/social studies texts in the grades 6-8 text complexity band independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand history and social studies texts for grades 6-8 on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the relationship between a primary and secondary source on the same topic.
description_plain: Your child can compare and explain how a primary source and a secondary source on the same topic relate to each other.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish among fact, opinion, and reasoned judgment in a text.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between facts, opinions, and thoughtful judgments in what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate visual information (e.g., in charts, graphs, photographs, videos, or maps) with other information in print and digital texts.
description_plain: Your child can use information from pictures, charts, graphs, videos, or maps together with written or digital texts to understand a topic better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify aspects of a text that reveal an author's point of view or purpose (e.g., loaded language, inclusion or avoidance of particular facts).
description_plain: Your child can spot parts of a text that show the author's point of view or purpose, like strong language or what facts are included or left out.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how a text presents information (e.g., sequentially, comparatively, causally)
description_plain: Your child can describe how a text shares information, like in order, by comparing, or showing cause and effect.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including vocabulary specific to domains related to history/social studies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in history or social studies texts, including special vocabulary.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify key steps in a text's description of a process related to history/social studies (e.g., how a bill becomes law, how interest rates are raised or lowered).
description_plain: Your child can identify the important steps in a history or social studies process described in a text, like how a bill becomes a law.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the central ideas or information of a primary or secondary source; provide an accurate summary of the source distinct from prior knowledge or opinions.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or important information in a primary or secondary source and give a clear summary without adding personal opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite specific textual evidence to support analysis of primary and secondary sources.
description_plain: Your child can point to exact parts of primary and secondary sources to support their analysis.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and accurately use grade-appropriate general academic and domain-specific words and phrases; gather vocabulary knowledge when considering a word or phrase important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child can learn and use new academic and subject-specific words correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish among the connotations (associations) of words with similar denotations (definitions) (e.g., stingy, scrimping, economical, unwasteful, thrifty).
description_plain: Your child understands the different feelings or ideas that similar words can have.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the relationship between particular words (e.g., cause/effect, part/whole, item/category) to better understand each of the words.
description_plain: Your child uses the relationship between words, like cause and effect or part and whole, to understand their meanings better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret the intent or meaning of figures of speech (e.g., personification, metaphors, alliteration) as used in context.
description_plain: Your child can understand the meaning of figures of speech like metaphors or personification when reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands figurative language, how words relate to each other, and subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Verify the preliminary determination of the meaning of a word or phrase (e.g., by checking the inferred meaning in context or in a dictionary).
description_plain: Your child checks the meaning of a word by looking at how it’s used or by using a dictionary.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult reference materials (e.g., dictionaries, glossaries, thesauruses and specialized reference materials), both print and digital, to find the pronunciation of a word or determine or clarify its precise meaning or its part of speech.
description_plain: Your child can use dictionaries, glossaries, or thesauruses to find how to say a word or what it means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use common, grade-appropriate Greek or Latin affixes and roots as clues to the meaning of a word (e.g., audience, auditory, audible).
description_plain: Your child uses common Greek or Latin roots and prefixes to help figure out what words mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine meaning of unfamiliar words by using knowledge of word structure (root words, prefixes, suffixes, abbreviations) and language structure through reading words in text (word order, grammar), context (e.g., the overall meaning of a sentence or paragraph; a word's position or function in a sentence) as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of new words by looking at parts of the word and how it is used in a sentence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade 6 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of unknown or multiple-meaning words using different strategies while reading or learning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Maintain consistency in style and tone.
description_plain: Your child keeps the same style and tone when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Vary sentence patterns for meaning, reader/listener interest, and style.
description_plain: Your child changes sentence types to make writing more interesting and clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.
description_plain: Your child knows and uses the rules of language when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use punctuation (commas, parentheses, dashes) to set off nonrestrictive/parenthetical elements.
description_plain: Your child uses commas, parentheses, or dashes to add extra information in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize variations from standard English in their own and others' writing and speaking, and identify and use strategies to improve expression in conventional language.
description_plain: Your child notices when language is not standard and learns ways to improve their writing and speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and correct vague pronouns (i.e., ones with unclear or ambiguous antecedents).
description_plain: Your child finds and fixes unclear pronouns that don’t clearly show who or what they mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and correct inappropriate shifts in pronoun number and person.
description_plain: Your child notices and corrects when pronouns change in number or person inappropriately.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use intensive pronouns (e.g., myself, ourselves).
description_plain: Your child uses words like 'myself' or 'ourselves' to emphasize who is doing something.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ensure that pronouns are in the proper case (subjective, objective, possessive).
description_plain: Your child uses pronouns correctly as subjects, objects, or to show possession.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses correct English grammar and language rules when writing or speaking and builds on what they learned before.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate. (See grade 6 Language standards 1 and 3 for specific expectations.)
description_plain: Your child changes how they speak depending on the situation and uses formal English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Include multimedia components (e.g., graphics, images, music, sound) and visual displays in presentations to clarify information.
description_plain: Your child adds pictures, music, or other media to presentations to help explain their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present claims and findings, sequencing ideas logically and using pertinent descriptions, facts, and details to accentuate main ideas or themes; use appropriate eye contact, adequate volume, and clear pronunciation.
description_plain: Your child shares ideas clearly and in order, using facts and details, while speaking with good eye contact and volume.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate a speaker's argument and specific claims, distinguishing claims that are supported by reasons and evidence from claims that are not.
description_plain: Your child identifies a speaker’s main argument and knows which claims have good reasons and evidence and which do not.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret information presented in diverse media (included but not limited to podcasts) and formats (e.g., visually, quantitatively/data-related, orally) and explain how it contributes to a topic, text, or issue under study.
description_plain: Your child can understand information from different media like podcasts or charts and explain how it relates to a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Review the key ideas expressed and demonstrate understanding of multiple perspectives through reflection and paraphrasing.
description_plain: Your child can think about and explain different viewpoints by summarizing what others say.

subject: Language Arts
description: Pose and respond to specific questions with elaboration and detail by making comments that contribute to the topic, text, or issue under discussion.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer detailed questions to add to a discussion about a topic or text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow rules for collegial discussions (e.g., establishing norms: taking turns, paraphrasing, respecting diverse viewpoints), set specific goals and deadlines, and define individual roles as needed.
description_plain: Your child can follow rules for group discussions, like taking turns and respecting others' ideas, and help set goals and roles.

subject: Language Arts
description: Come to discussions prepared, having read or studied required material; explicitly draw on that preparation by referring to evidence on the topic, text, or issue to probe and reflect on ideas under discussion.
description_plain: Your child comes to discussions prepared by reading or studying, and uses facts from that preparation to ask questions and think about ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher-led) with diverse partners on grade 6 topics, texts, and issues, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly.
description_plain: Your child takes part in different kinds of group discussions, listens to others, and shares their own ideas clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child writes regularly over short and long periods for different subjects, purposes, and audiences, including time for research and revision.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply grade 6 Reading standards to literary nonfiction (e.g., "Trace and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, distinguishing claims that are supported by reasons and evidence from claims that are not").
description_plain: Your child reads nonfiction and can find and think about the reasons and evidence that support the author’s ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply grade 6 Reading standards to literature (e.g., "Compare and contrast texts in different forms or genres (e.g., stories and poems; historical novels and fantasy stories) in terms of their approaches to similar themes and topics.").
description_plain: Your child compares different types of stories or poems to see how they handle similar themes or topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child can find and use details from stories or information texts to support their thoughts, reflections, or research.
