subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources; assess the credibility of each source; and quote or paraphrase the data and conclusions of others while avoiding plagiarism and providing basic bibliographic information for sources.
description_plain: Your child can find information from books and websites, decide if the sources are trustworthy, and use the information correctly without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects to answer a question, drawing on several sources and refocusing the inquiry when appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can do short research projects by using several sources and changing their questions if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing as well as to interact and collaborate with others; demonstrate sufficient command of keyboarding skills to create a piece of writing.
description_plain: Your child can use computers and the Internet to write, share their work, and work with others, while typing well enough to do these tasks.

subject: Language Arts
description: With some guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach. (Editing for conventions should demonstrate command of Language standards 1–3 up to and including grade 6.)
description_plain: With some help from others, your child can plan, revise, edit, and improve their writing or try a new way of writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience. (Grade-specific expectations for writing types are defined in standards 1–3 above.)
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and in an organized way that fits the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a conclusion that follows from the narrated experiences or events.
description_plain: Your child writes a conclusion that follows naturally from the story or events they described.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise words and phrases, relevant descriptive details, and sensory language to convey experiences and events.
description_plain: Your child uses exact words, clear details, and descriptions that appeal to the senses to tell about experiences and events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a variety of transition words, phrases, and clauses to convey sequence and signal shifts from one time frame or setting to another.
description_plain: Your child can use different words and phrases to show the order of events and changes in time or place in their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use narrative techniques, such as dialogue, pacing, and description, to develop experiences, events, and/or characters.
description_plain: Your child will use dialogue, pacing, and descriptions to make stories and characters more interesting and clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage and orient the reader by establishing a context and introducing a narrator and/or characters; organize an event sequence that unfolds naturally and logically.
description_plain: Your child starts a story by setting the scene, introducing characters or narrator, and organizing events in order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use narrative writing to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, relevant descriptive details, and well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or imagined events with good details and clear order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from the information or explanation presented.
description_plain: Your child writes a conclusion that sums up the information or explanation they gave.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a formal style.
description_plain: Your child writes in a formal style when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to inform about or explain the topic.
description_plain: Your child uses clear and specific words related to the topic to explain or inform.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use appropriate transitions to clarify the relationships among ideas and concepts.
description_plain: Your child uses transition words to show how ideas and concepts connect.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with relevant facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations, or other information and examples.
description_plain: Your child can add facts, definitions, details, quotes, or examples to explain a topic clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic; organize ideas, concepts, and information, using strategies such as definition, classification, comparison/contrast, and cause/effect; include formatting (e.g., headings), graphics (e.g., charts, tables), and multimedia when useful to aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child will introduce a topic and organize information using methods like comparing or explaining causes, and use headings, charts, or pictures to help readers understand.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine a topic and convey ideas, concepts, and information through the selection, organization, and analysis of relevant content.
description_plain: Your child can write informative texts that explain a topic by choosing and organizing important facts and ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from the argument presented.
description_plain: Your child writes a conclusion that follows from the argument they made.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a formal style.
description_plain: Your child writes in a formal style when making an argument.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words, phrases, and clauses to clarify the relationships among claim(s) and reasons.
description_plain: Your child can use words and phrases to clearly show how their reasons support their main point.

subject: Language Arts
description: Support claim(s) with clear reasons and accurate, relevant evidence, using credible sources and demonstrating an understanding of the topic or text.
description_plain: Your child can support their opinions with clear reasons and accurate evidence from trustworthy sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce claim(s) and organize the reasons and evidence clearly.
description_plain: Your child introduces their claims and organizes reasons and evidence clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims with clear reasons and relevant evidence.
description_plain: Your child can write arguments that support their claims with clear reasons and relevant evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literary nonfiction, within a complexity band appropriate to grade 6 (from upper grade 5 to grade 7), with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand nonfiction texts for grade 6, with help if needed for harder parts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast one author's presentation of events with that of another (e.g., a memoir written by and a biography on the same person).
description_plain: Your child can compare how two different authors tell the same story or event.

subject: Language Arts
description: Trace and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, distinguishing claims that are supported by reasons and evidence from claims that are not.
description_plain: Your child can find and think about the reasons and evidence an author uses to support their ideas, and tell which ideas are not supported.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate information presented in different media (e.g., may include, but not limited to podcasts) or formats (e.g., visually, quantitatively/data-related) as well as in words to develop a coherent understanding of a topic or issue.
description_plain: Your child can combine information from different sources like podcasts, pictures, and text to understand a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine an author's purpose (to inform, persuade, entertain, critique, etc.) and point of view in a text and explain how it is conveyed in the text.
description_plain: Your child can figure out why an author wrote a text and what their point of view is, and explain how the text shows this.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how a particular sentence, paragraph, chapter, or section fits into the overall structure of a text and contributes to the development of the ideas.
description_plain: Your child can explain how a sentence, paragraph, or section fits into the whole text and helps develop the main ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative, connotative, and technical meanings.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a text, including special or creative meanings, and explain how word choice changes the meaning or feeling.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze in detail how a key individual, event, or idea is introduced, illustrated, and elaborated on in a text (e.g., through examples or anecdotes).
description_plain: Your child can explain in detail how a person, event, or idea is introduced and explained in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine a central idea and subtopics of a text and how they are conveyed through particular details; restate and summarize the central idea or events, in correct sequence when necessary, after reading a text.
description_plain: Your child can find the main idea and subtopics of a text, explain them with details, and summarize the text in order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can find and use parts of the text to support what the text says clearly and what can be understood by thinking about it.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend a range of literature from a variety of cultures, within a complexity band appropriate to grade 6 (from upper grade 5 to grade 7), with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand stories from different cultures at a sixth-grade level.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast texts in different forms or genres (e.g., stories and poems; historical novels and fantasy stories) in terms of their approaches to similar themes and topics.
description_plain: Your child can compare stories or poems that are different types but talk about similar ideas or themes.

subject: Language Arts
description: (Not applicable to literature)
description_plain: This standard does not apply to literature.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the experience of reading a story, drama, or poem to listening to or viewing an audio, video, or live version of the text, including contrasting what they "see" and "hear" when reading the text to what they perceive when they listen or watch (e.g., Shiloh).
description_plain: Your child can compare reading a story, play, or poem to listening to or watching it, noticing differences in what they see and hear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine author's purpose and explain how an author develops the point of view of the narrator or speaker in a text.
description_plain: Your child can figure out why an author wrote a story and explain how the author shows the narrator's or speaker's point of view.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the overall structure of a text: how a particular sentence, chapter, scene, or stanza fits into the overall work and analyzing how it contributes to the development of the characters, theme, setting, or plot.
description_plain: Your child can look at how parts of a story, like a sentence or chapter, fit into the whole story and help develop characters, theme, setting, or plot.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative and connotative meanings. Analyze the impact of a specific word choice on meaning and tone.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in stories or poems, including special meanings and sounds, and explain how word choice affects the mood or feeling.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how a particular story's or drama's plot unfolds in a series of episodes as well as how the characters respond or change as the plot moves toward a resolution or as the narrative advances.
description_plain: Your child can describe how the plot of a story or play happens in parts and how the characters change or react as the story moves toward the ending.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine a theme or central idea of a text and how it is conveyed through particular details; restate and summarize main ideas or events, in correct sequence, after reading a text.
description_plain: Your child can find the main message or theme of a story and explain it using details, then retell the main ideas or events in the right order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can find and use parts of the story or poem to support what it says clearly and what can be understood by thinking about it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately a range of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college- and career-readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge when encountering an unknown term important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child can learn and use a wide range of words for reading, writing, speaking, and listening, and can figure out new words on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands figurative language, how words relate to each other, and subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases by using context clues, analyzing meaningful word parts, and consulting general and specialized reference materials, as appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of unknown or tricky words by using clues in the sentence, looking at parts of the word, or checking dictionaries.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child understands how language works in different situations and can choose words and styles that fit what they want to say or understand better when reading or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and word usage when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and communicative tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can change how they speak depending on the situation and use formal English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make strategic use of digital media and visual displays of data to express information and enhance understanding of presentations.
description_plain: Your child can use digital tools and pictures to share information and help others understand their presentations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present information, findings, and supporting evidence such that listeners can follow the line of reasoning and that the organization, development, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can share information and evidence clearly so listeners can follow their ideas and understand the purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate a speaker's point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric.
description_plain: Your child can think about and judge a speaker’s opinions, reasons, and how they use evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child can combine and think about information from different types of media like videos, numbers, and spoken words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Prepare for and participate effectively in a range of conversations and collaborations with diverse partners, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly and persuasively.
description_plain: Your child can prepare for and take part in conversations with different people, building on others’ ideas and sharing their own clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child writes regularly over short and long periods for different reasons and audiences, including time for research and revising.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child can find and use evidence from stories or informational texts to support their thinking and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, assess the credibility and accuracy of each source, and integrate the information while avoiding plagiarism.
description_plain: Your child can gather information from many sources, check if the sources are trustworthy, and use the information without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short as well as more sustained research projects based on focused questions, demonstrating understanding of the subject under investigation.
description_plain: Your child can do short and longer research projects based on specific questions and show they understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child can use technology like the Internet to write, publish their work, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach.
description_plain: Your child can plan, revise, edit, and rewrite their writing to make it better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and organize their ideas in a way that fits the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use narrative writing to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, well-chosen details, and well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or imagined events using good details and clear order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine and convey complex ideas and information clearly and accurately through the effective selection, organization, and analysis of content.
description_plain: Your child can write informative texts that explain complex ideas clearly by choosing and organizing important information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims in an analysis of substantive topics or texts, using valid reasoning and relevant and sufficient evidence.
description_plain: Your child can write arguments that support their opinions with good reasons and evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend a range of complex literary and informational texts independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand difficult stories and informational texts on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can analyze how two or more texts on similar topics build knowledge or show different author approaches.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can find and explain the main argument and claims in a text, checking if the reasons and evidence make sense and are enough.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can combine and think about information from different types of media, like pictures, numbers, and words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child understands how the author's point of view or purpose changes what the text says and how it is written.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of the text (e.g., a section, chapter, scene, or stanza) relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can look at how sentences, paragraphs, and bigger parts of a text fit together and connect to the whole story or article.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text, including determining technical, connotative, and figurative meanings, and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a text, including special or hidden meanings, and explain how word choices affect the feeling or message.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, and ideas grow and connect throughout a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas or themes of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or themes in a text, explain how they develop, and summarize important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what the text says explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child reads carefully to understand what the text says and can make smart guesses, using examples from the text to support their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words and phrases acquired through conversations, reading and being read to, and responding to texts, including using frequently occurring conjunctions to signal simple relationships (e.g., because).
description_plain: Your child uses new words and phrases learned from talking, reading, and listening, including words like 'because' to show simple ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish shades of meaning among verbs differing in manner (e.g., look, peek, glance, stare, glare, scowl) and adjectives differing in intensity (e.g., large, gigantic) by defining or choosing them or by acting out the meanings.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between similar action words and describing words by explaining, choosing, or acting them out.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify real-life connections between words and their use (e.g., note places at home that are cozy).
description_plain: Your child can notice how words relate to real life, like pointing out cozy places at home.

subject: Language Arts
description: Define words by category and by one or more key attributes (e.g., a duck is a bird that swims; a tiger is a large cat with stripes).
description_plain: Your child can explain what words mean by naming their group and important details, like saying a duck is a bird that swims.

subject: Language Arts
description: Sort words into categories (e.g., colors, clothing) to gain a sense of the concepts the categories represent.
description_plain: Your child can sort words into groups, like colors or clothes, to understand what those groups mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, demonstrate understanding of word relationships and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: With help, your child shows they understand how words relate to each other and have different meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify frequently occurring root words (e.g., look) and their inflectional forms (e.g., looks, looked, looking).
description_plain: Your child can recognize common root words and their different forms, like 'look,' 'looks,' and 'looking.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Use frequently occurring affixes as a clue to the meaning of a word.
description_plain: Your child uses common beginnings and endings of words to help figure out their meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use sentence-level context as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can use the sentence around a word to help understand what the word means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade 1 reading and content, choosing flexibly from an array of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of new or tricky words by using different strategies while reading or learning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell untaught words phonetically, drawing on phonemic awareness and spelling conventions.
description_plain: Your child can spell new words by sounding them out and using spelling rules they know.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use conventional spelling for words with common spelling patterns and for frequently occurring irregular words.
description_plain: Your child spells common words and tricky words correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commas in dates and to separate single words in a series.
description_plain: Your child uses commas correctly in dates and when listing words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use end punctuation for sentences.
description_plain: Your child uses periods, question marks, and exclamation points at the end of sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Capitalize dates and names of people.
description_plain: Your child capitalizes the names of people and dates.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce and expand complete simple and compound declarative, interrogative, imperative, and exclamatory sentences in response to prompts.
description_plain: Your child can make and expand simple and compound sentences that tell, ask, command, or show strong feelings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use frequently occurring prepositions (e.g., during, beyond, toward).
description_plain: Your child uses common words that show location or time, like during, beyond, and toward.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use determiners (e.g., articles, demonstratives).
description_plain: Your child uses words like a, an, the, this, and that to point out nouns.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use frequently occurring conjunctions (e.g., and, but, or, so, because).
description_plain: Your child uses common connecting words like and, but, or, so, and because.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use frequently occurring adjectives.
description_plain: Your child uses common describing words (adjectives).

subject: Language Arts
description: Use verbs to convey a sense of past, present, and future (e.g., Yesterday I walked home; Today I walk home; Tomorrow I will walk home).
description_plain: Your child uses verbs to talk about things that happened in the past, are happening now, or will happen in the future.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use personal, possessive, and indefinite pronouns (e.g., I me, my; they, them, their, anyone, everything).
description_plain: Your child can use words like I, me, my, they, them, their, anyone, and everything correctly when speaking and writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use singular and plural nouns with matching verbs in basic sentences (e.g., He hops; We hop).
description_plain: Your child uses singular and plural nouns with matching verbs in simple sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use common, proper, and possessive nouns.
description_plain: Your child uses common nouns, names of people or places, and shows possession with nouns.

subject: Language Arts
description: Print all upper- and lowercase letters.
description_plain: Your child can print all uppercase and lowercase letters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and language rules when speaking and writing, building on what they learned before.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce complete sentences when appropriate to task and situation. (See grade 1 Language standards 1 and 3 for specific expectations.)
description_plain: Your child can say complete sentences when it fits the situation or task.

subject: Language Arts
description: Add drawings or other visual displays to descriptions when appropriate to clarify ideas, thoughts, and feelings.
description_plain: Your child adds drawings or pictures to help explain ideas, thoughts, and feelings when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe people, places, things, and events with relevant details, expressing ideas and feelings clearly.
description_plain: Your child describes people, places, things, and events with details, using clear words to share ideas and feelings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about what a speaker says in order to gather additional information or clarify something that is not understood.
description_plain: Your child asks and answers questions about what someone says to learn more or understand better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about key details in a text read aloud or information presented orally or through other media.
description_plain: Your child asks and answers questions about important details in stories or information they hear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask questions to clear up any confusion about the topics and texts under discussion.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions to understand better when something is confusing during talks about stories or topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Build on others' talk in conversations by responding to the comments of others through multiple exchanges.
description_plain: Your child will join conversations by listening and responding to what others say, having back-and-forth talks.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow agreed-upon rules for discussions (e.g., listening to others with care, speaking one at a time about the topics and texts under discussion).
description_plain: Your child understands and follows simple rules for talking with others, like listening carefully and taking turns to speak.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in collaborative conversations with diverse partners about grade 1 topics and texts with peers and adults in small and larger groups.
description_plain: Your child will take part in group talks with classmates and adults about first-grade topics and stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, recall information from experiences or gather information from provided sources to answer a question.
description_plain: With help, your child can remember or find information from experiences or sources to answer questions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in shared research and writing projects (e.g., explore a number of "how-to" books on a given topic and use them to write a sequence of instructions or combine or summarize information/facts learned).
description_plain: Your child will work with others to research and write projects, like reading 'how-to' books and writing steps to do something.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, use a variety of digital tools to produce and publish writing, including in collaboration with peers.
description_plain: With support, your child can use computers or other tools to write and share their work, learning to print, write cursive, or type.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, focus on a topic, respond to questions and suggestions from peers, and add concrete and sensory details to strengthen writing as needed.
description_plain: With guidance, your child focuses on a topic, listens to ideas from friends, and adds details to make their writing better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use narrative writing to recount two or more real or imagined sequenced events, include some details regarding what happened, who was there, use linking words to signal event order (e.g., first, next, then), and provide an ending (e.g., how the problem was solved; how someone felt at the end).
description_plain: Your child can write a story about two or more real or made-up events, include details about what happened and who was there, use words like first and next to show order, and finish with an ending.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts in which they name a topic, supply some facts about the topic, and provide some sense of closure (e.g., restate at the end the most interesting fact or the most important idea shared).
description_plain: Your child can write to explain a topic by naming it, giving some facts, and ending the writing clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write opinion pieces in which they introduce the topic or name the book they are writing about, state an opinion or preference, supply a fact (e.g., because race cars go faster than…) or reason for the opinion, and end with a sentence that restates their opinion related to a feeling or emotion (e.g., it makes me laugh; that was the scariest part).
description_plain: Your child can write their opinion by saying the topic or book, giving a reason for their opinion, and ending their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, rereading as necessary.
description_plain: Your child uses clues from the sentence to figure out or fix words while reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read on-level text orally with accuracy, appropriate rate, and expression on successive readings.
description_plain: Your child reads grade-level text out loud accurately, at a good speed, and with expression.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read on-level text with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child reads grade-level text with understanding and purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read with sufficient accuracy and fluency to support comprehension.
description_plain: Your child reads accurately and smoothly enough to understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and read grade-appropriate irregularly spelled words.
description_plain: Your child recognizes and reads common tricky words that don’t follow usual spelling rules.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read words with inflectional endings.
description_plain: Your child reads words with endings that change their meaning, like -ed or -s.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode two-syllable words following basic patterns by breaking the words into syllables.
description_plain: Your child breaks two-syllable words into parts to read them.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge that every syllable must have a vowel sound to determine the number of syllables in a printed word.
description_plain: Your child knows that every syllable has a vowel sound and uses this to count syllables in words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know final -e and common vowel team conventions for representing long vowel sounds.
description_plain: Your child knows that a silent e or vowel pairs can make a long vowel sound.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode regularly spelled one-syllable words.
description_plain: Your child can read one-syllable words that follow common spelling rules.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know the spelling-sound correspondences for common consonant digraphs.
description_plain: Your child knows how to read common two-letter sounds like ch, sh, and th.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words.
description_plain: Your child uses phonics and word skills to read words at their grade level.

subject: Language Arts
description: Segment spoken single-syllable words into their complete sequence of individual sounds (phonemes).
description_plain: Your child can say all the individual sounds in a one-syllable word.

subject: Language Arts
description: Isolate and pronounce initial, medial vowel, and final sounds (phonemes) in spoken single-syllable words.
description_plain: Your child can say the first, middle vowel, and last sounds in a one-syllable word.

subject: Language Arts
description: Orally produce single-syllable words by blending sounds (phonemes), including consonant blends.
description_plain: Your child can say a one-syllable word by putting together sounds, including blends.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish long from short vowel sounds in spoken single-syllable words.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between long and short vowel sounds in spoken one-syllable words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of spoken words, syllables, and sounds (phonemes).
description_plain: Your child understands spoken words, syllables, and sounds.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize the distinguishing features of a sentence (e.g., first word, capitalization, ending punctuation).
description_plain: Your child knows what makes a sentence, like starting with a capital letter and ending with punctuation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of the organization and basic features of print.
description_plain: Your child understands how print works and the basic parts of books and texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, read informational texts on a range of topics appropriately complex for grade 1, with scaffolding as needed.
description_plain: With help, your child can read simple informational books on different topics that are right for first grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify basic similarities in and differences between information presented in two texts on the same topic (e.g., compare two graphics, descriptions, or steps in a process to make something).
description_plain: Your child can find basic ways two texts about the same topic are alike and different, like comparing pictures or steps in how to do something.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the opinions an author states to support points in a text.
description_plain: Your child can find the opinions an author shares to support their ideas in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the illustrations and details in a text to describe its key ideas.
description_plain: Your child uses pictures and details in a text to explain the main ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish between information provided by photos or other graphics and information provided by the words in a text.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between information shown in pictures or graphics and information written in the words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and use various text features (e.g., title, labels with graphics, bold print, visual cues such as arrows, electronic menus, icons) to locate key facts or information in a text.
description_plain: Your child can use parts of a book like the title, labels, bold words, arrows, or icons to find important facts or information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions to help determine or clarify the meaning of words and phrases in a text.
description_plain: Your child asks and answers questions to understand the meaning of words and phrases in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the connection between two individuals, events, ideas, or pieces of information in a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how two people, events, ideas, or facts in a text are connected.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the main topic or author's purpose (e.g., to teach or tell us about …) and retell key details of a text.
description_plain: Your child can tell what the main topic or reason for a text is and retell important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, elicit background/prior knowledge and experience in order to ask and answer questions about an informational text using key details from the text.
description_plain: With help, your child can use what they already know to ask and answer questions about an informational text using important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, read prose and poetry from a variety of cultures of appropriate complexity for grade 1.
description_plain: With help, your child can read stories and poems from different cultures that are right for first grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, compare and contrast the adventures and experiences of characters in stories.
description_plain: With help, your child can compare and talk about how characters in different stories have similar or different adventures and experiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: (Not applicable to literature)
description_plain: This standard does not apply to literature at this grade level.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use illustrations and details in a story read or read aloud to describe its characters, events, setting, or problem-solution.
description_plain: Your child can use pictures and details from a story to describe the characters, events, setting, or how problems are solved.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify who is telling the story at various points in the text (e.g., a character in the text or a narrator/story teller).
description_plain: Your child can tell who is telling the story at different parts, like a character or a narrator.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain major differences between books that tell stories and books that give information (fiction, non-fiction, and poetry), drawing on a wide reading of a range of text types.
description_plain: Your child can explain the main differences between storybooks and books that give information, like fiction, non-fiction, and poetry.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify words and phrases in stories, plays, or poems that suggest feelings or appeal to the senses.
description_plain: Your child can find words and phrases in stories, plays, or poems that show feelings or help you imagine what is happening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe characters, settings, major events, and problem-solution in a story, play, or poem, using key details.
description_plain: Your child can describe characters, places, important events, and how problems get solved in a story, play, or poem using key details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Retell stories, using key details, and demonstrate understanding their message (e.g., teach a lesson, make you laugh, tell a scary story, tell about an event) or lesson.
description_plain: Your child can retell stories using important details and show they understand the story’s message or lesson.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about a literary text using key details from the text.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about a story using important details from the story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately a range of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college- and career-readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge when encountering an unknown term important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child can learn and use a wide range of words for reading, writing, speaking, and listening, and can figure out new words on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands figurative language, how words relate to each other, and subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases by using context clues, analyzing meaningful word parts, and consulting general and specialized reference materials, as appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of unknown or tricky words by using clues in the sentence, looking at parts of the word, or checking dictionaries.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child understands how language works in different situations and can choose words and styles that fit what they want to say or understand better when reading or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and word usage when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and communicative tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can change how they speak depending on the situation and use formal English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make strategic use of digital media and visual displays of data to express information and enhance understanding of presentations.
description_plain: Your child can use digital tools and pictures to share information and help others understand their presentations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present information, findings, and supporting evidence such that listeners can follow the line of reasoning and that the organization, development, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can share information and evidence clearly so listeners can follow their ideas and understand the purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate a speaker's point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric.
description_plain: Your child can think about and judge a speaker’s opinions, reasons, and how they use evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child can combine and think about information from different types of media like videos, numbers, and spoken words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Prepare for and participate effectively in a range of conversations and collaborations with diverse partners, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly and persuasively.
description_plain: Your child can prepare for and take part in conversations with different people, building on others’ ideas and sharing their own clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child writes regularly over short and long periods for different reasons and audiences, including time for research and revising.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child can find and use evidence from stories or informational texts to support their thinking and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, assess the credibility and accuracy of each source, and integrate the information while avoiding plagiarism.
description_plain: Your child can gather information from many sources, check if the sources are trustworthy, and use the information without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short as well as more sustained research projects based on focused questions, demonstrating understanding of the subject under investigation.
description_plain: Your child can do short and longer research projects based on specific questions and show they understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child can use technology like the Internet to write, publish their work, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach.
description_plain: Your child can plan, revise, edit, and rewrite their writing to make it better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and organize their ideas in a way that fits the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use narrative writing to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, well-chosen details, and well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or imagined events using good details and clear order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine and convey complex ideas and information clearly and accurately through the effective selection, organization, and analysis of content.
description_plain: Your child can write informative texts that explain complex ideas clearly by choosing and organizing important information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims in an analysis of substantive topics or texts, using valid reasoning and relevant and sufficient evidence.
description_plain: Your child can write arguments that support their opinions with good reasons and evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend a range of complex literary and informational texts independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand difficult stories and informational texts on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can analyze how two or more texts on similar topics build knowledge or show different author approaches.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can find and explain the main argument and claims in a text, checking if the reasons and evidence make sense and are enough.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can combine and think about information from different types of media, like pictures, numbers, and words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child understands how the author's point of view or purpose changes what the text says and how it is written.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of the text (e.g., a section, chapter, scene, or stanza) relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can look at how sentences, paragraphs, and bigger parts of a text fit together and connect to the whole story or article.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text, including determining technical, connotative, and figurative meanings, and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a text, including special or hidden meanings, and explain how word choices affect the feeling or message.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, and ideas grow and connect throughout a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas or themes of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or themes in a text, explain how they develop, and summarize important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what the text says explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child reads carefully to understand what the text says and can make smart guesses, using examples from the text to support their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for reflection and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child can write regularly over short and long times for different school subjects, purposes, and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child uses evidence from informational texts to support their analysis, reflection, and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, using search terms effectively; assess the credibility and accuracy of each source; and quote or paraphrase the data and conclusions of others while avoiding plagiarism and following a standard format for citation.
description_plain: Your child can find useful information from books and online, check if sources are trustworthy, and use others' ideas correctly without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects to answer a question (including a self-generated question), drawing on several sources and generating additional related, focused questions that allow for multiple avenues of exploration.
description_plain: Your child can do short research projects to answer questions, using several sources and asking more focused questions to explore.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and present the relationships between information and ideas clearly and efficiently.
description_plain: Your child can use technology like the Internet to write and share their work clearly and effectively.

subject: Language Arts
description: With some guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach, focusing on how well purpose and audience have been addressed.
description_plain: With some help, your child can improve their writing by planning, revising, editing, or trying new ways to better reach their audience and purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and organized in a way that fits the task, purpose, and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Not applicable as a separate requirement.
description_plain: This standard is not a separate requirement.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the information or explanation presented.
description_plain: Your child can write a conclusion that clearly follows from and supports the information or explanation they gave.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a formal style and objective tone.
description_plain: Your child can write in a formal style and keep an objective tone.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to inform about or explain the topic.
description_plain: Your child can use clear and specific language and special vocabulary to explain a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use appropriate and varied transitions to create cohesion and clarify the relationships among ideas and concepts.
description_plain: Your child can use different transitions to connect ideas and make their writing clear and easy to follow.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with relevant facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations, or other information and examples.
description_plain: Your child can add facts, definitions, details, and quotes to explain a topic clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic clearly, previewing what is to follow; organize ideas, concepts, and information into broader categories as appropriate to achieving purpose; include formatting (e.g., headings) graphics (e.g., charts, tables), and multimedia when useful to aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can clearly introduce a topic, organize ideas into groups, and use headings, charts, or multimedia to help readers understand.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts, including the narration of historical events, scientific procedures/experiments, or technical processes.
description_plain: Your child can write informative texts that explain history events, science experiments, or technical steps.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the argument presented.
description_plain: Your child can write a conclusion that supports and follows from the argument they made.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a formal style.
description_plain: Your child can write in a formal style.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words, phrases, and clauses to create cohesion and clarify the relationships among claim(s), counterclaims, reasons, and evidence.
description_plain: Your child can use words and phrases to connect claims, opposing claims, reasons, and evidence clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Support claim(s) with logical reasoning and relevant, accurate data and evidence that demonstrate an understanding of the topic or text, using credible sources.
description_plain: Your child can support their claims with logical reasons and accurate facts from trustworthy sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce claim(s) abut a topic or issue, acknowledge and distinguish the claim(s) from alternate or opposing claims, and organize the reasons and evidence logically.
description_plain: Your child can introduce their opinion on a topic, recognize other opinions, and organize their reasons and evidence clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments focused on discipline-specific content.
description_plain: Your child can write arguments about topics in specific school subjects.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of grade 8, read and comprehend science/technical texts in the grades 6-8 text complexity band independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand science and technical texts for grades 6 to 8 on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the information gained from experiments, simulations, video, or multimedia sources with that gained from reading a text on the same topic.
description_plain: Your child can compare information from experiments, videos, or simulations with what they read about the same topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish among facts, reasoned judgment based on research findings, and speculation in a text.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between facts, opinions based on research, and guesses in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate quantitative or technical information expressed in words in a text with a version of that information expressed visually (e.g., in a flowchart, diagram, model, graph, or table).
description_plain: Your child can combine information given in words with pictures, charts, or graphs to understand a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analysis the author's purpose in providing an explanation, describing a procedure, or discussing an experiment in a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain why an author gives instructions, describes a process, or talks about an experiment.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure an author uses to organize a text, including how the major sections contribute to the whole and to an understanding of the topic.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author organizes a text and how its parts help explain the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of symbols, key terms, and other domain-specific words and phrases as they are used in a specific scientific or technical context relevant to grades 6-8 texts and topics.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what symbols and special words mean when reading science or technical texts for grades 6–8.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow precisely a multistep procedure when carrying out experiments, taking measurements, or performing technical tasks.
description_plain: Your child can carefully follow multi-step instructions when doing experiments, measuring, or technical tasks.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the central ideas or conclusions of a text; provide an accurate summary of the text distinct from prior knowledge or opinions.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or conclusions in a text and give a clear summary without mixing in their own opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite specific textual evidence to support analysis of science and technical texts.
description_plain: Your child can point to exact parts of a science or technical text to support their understanding and analysis.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of grade 8, read and comprehend history/social studies texts in the grades 6-8 text complexity band independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand history and social studies texts for grades 6-8 on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the relationship between a primary and secondary source on the same topic.
description_plain: Your child can compare and explain how a primary source and a secondary source on the same topic relate to each other.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish among fact, opinion, and reasoned judgment in a text.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between facts, opinions, and thoughtful judgments in what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate visual information (e.g., in charts, graphs, photographs, videos, or maps) with other information in print and digital texts.
description_plain: Your child can use information from pictures, charts, graphs, videos, or maps together with written or digital texts to understand a topic better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify aspects of a text that reveal an author's point of view or purpose (e.g., loaded language, inclusion or avoidance of particular facts).
description_plain: Your child can spot parts of a text that show the author's point of view or purpose, like strong language or what facts are included or left out.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how a text presents information (e.g., sequentially, comparatively, causally)
description_plain: Your child can describe how a text shares information, like in order, by comparing, or showing cause and effect.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including vocabulary specific to domains related to history/social studies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in history or social studies texts, including special vocabulary.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify key steps in a text's description of a process related to history/social studies (e.g., how a bill becomes law, how interest rates are raised or lowered).
description_plain: Your child can identify the important steps in a history or social studies process described in a text, like how a bill becomes a law.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the central ideas or information of a primary or secondary source; provide an accurate summary of the source distinct from prior knowledge or opinions.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or important information in a primary or secondary source and give a clear summary without adding personal opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite specific textual evidence to support analysis of primary and secondary sources.
description_plain: Your child can point to exact parts of primary and secondary sources to support their analysis.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and accurately use grade-appropriate general academic and domain-specific words and phrases; gather vocabulary knowledge when considering a word or phrase important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child will learn and use new words that are appropriate for 8th grade to help understand and express ideas better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish among the connotations (associations) of words with similar denotations (definitions) (e.g., bullheaded, willful, firm, persistent, resolute).
description_plain: Your child understands the different feelings or ideas that similar words can have, like how 'firm' and 'willful' mean different things even if they seem related.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the relationship between particular words (e.g., synonyms/antonyms, analogies) to better understand each of the words.
description_plain: Your child can use relationships between words, like synonyms and antonyms, to understand their meanings better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret the intent or meaning of figures of speech (e.g., verbal irony, puns, mixed metaphor) as used in context.
description_plain: Your child understands the meaning behind figures of speech like jokes, irony, or mixed metaphors when reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands figurative language, how words relate, and subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Verify the preliminary determination of the meaning of a word or phrase (e.g., by checking the inferred meaning in context or in a dictionary).
description_plain: Your child can check the meaning of a word or phrase by looking at how it's used or by using a dictionary.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult general and specialized reference materials (e.g., dictionaries, glossaries, and thesauruses), both print and digital, to find the pronunciation of a word or determine or clarify its precise meaning or its part of speech.
description_plain: Your child can use dictionaries, glossaries, and thesauruses, both in books and online, to find how to say a word or what it means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use common, grade-appropriate Greek or Latin affixes and roots as clues to the meaning of a word (e.g., precede, recede, secede).
description_plain: Your child can use common Greek or Latin beginnings and endings to help figure out what a word means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine meanings of unfamiliar words by using knowledge of word structure, (prefixes/suffixes, base words, common roots, or word origins), context (e.g., the overall meaning of a sentence or paragraph; a word's position or function in a sentence), knowledge of language structure including using context clues and prior knowledge.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of new words by looking at parts of the word, the sentence around it, and what they already know.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words or phrases based on grade 8 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.
description_plain: Your child figures out the meaning of new or tricky words by using different strategies based on what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use verbs in the active and passive voice and in the conditional and subjunctive mood to achieve particular effects (e.g., emphasizing the actor or the action; expressing uncertainty or describing a state contrary to fact).
description_plain: Your child can use different verb forms to focus on who is doing the action or to show uncertainty or imaginary situations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.
description_plain: Your child uses correct language rules when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use an ellipsis to indicate an omission.
description_plain: Your child can use three dots (an ellipsis) to show that some words have been left out.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use punctuation (comma, ellipsis, dash) to indicate a pause or break.
description_plain: Your child can use commas, ellipses, or dashes to show a pause or break in writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and correct inappropriate shifts in verb voice and mood.
description_plain: Your child can spot and fix mistakes when verb forms change in the wrong way.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use verbs in the indicative, imperative, interrogative, conditional, and subjunctive mood.
description_plain: Your child can use verbs in different moods like statements, commands, questions, or possibilities.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use verbs in the active and passive voice.
description_plain: Your child can use verbs that show who does the action and verbs that show the action is done to someone or something.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the function of verbals (gerunds, participles, infinitives) in general and their function in particular sentences in order to apply the conventions of English.
description_plain: Your child understands how verb forms like gerunds, participles, and infinitives work in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and language rules when writing or speaking and builds on what they learned before.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate. (See grade 8 Language standards 1 and 3 for specific expectations.)
description_plain: Your child changes how they speak depending on the situation and uses formal English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate multimedia and visual displays into presentations to clarify information, strengthen claims and evidence, and add interest.
description_plain: Your child adds pictures, videos, or other media to presentations to make ideas clearer and more interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present claims and findings, emphasizing salient points in a focused, coherent manner with relevant evidence, sound valid reasoning, and well-chosen details; use appropriate eye contact, adequate volume, and clear pronunciation.
description_plain: Your child presents ideas clearly and confidently, using good reasons, details, and appropriate words, while making eye contact and speaking loudly and clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate a speaker's argument and specific claims, evaluating the soundness of the reasoning and relevance and sufficiency of the evidence and identifying when irrelevant evidence is introduced.
description_plain: Your child explains a speaker’s argument and checks if the reasons and evidence make sense and if anything irrelevant is included.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the purpose of information presented in diverse media and formats (e.g., visually, quantitatively/data-related, orally) and evaluate the motives (e.g., social, commercial, political) behind its presentation.
description_plain: Your child looks at information from different sources and figures out why it was shared, like for social, business, or political reasons.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acknowledge new information expressed by others, and, when warranted, qualify or justify their own views in light of the evidence presented.
description_plain: Your child listens to new information from others and changes or explains their own ideas based on the evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Pose questions that connect the ideas of several speakers and respond to others' questions and comments with relevant evidence, observations, and ideas.
description_plain: Your child asks questions that connect ideas from different speakers and responds with relevant facts and thoughts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow rules for collegial discussions (e.g., establishing norms: taking turns, paraphrasing, respecting diverse viewpoints), and decision-making (e.g., coming to consensus), track progress toward specific goals and deadlines, and define individual roles as needed.
description_plain: Your child can follow rules for group discussions, like taking turns and respecting others' ideas, and help the group reach decisions and meet goals.

subject: Language Arts
description: Come to discussions prepared, having read or researched material under study; explicitly draw on that preparation by referring to evidence on the topic, text, or issue to probe and reflect on ideas under discussion.
description_plain: Your child comes to discussions prepared by reading or researching and uses evidence to explore and think about ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher-led) with diverse partners on grade 8 topics, texts, and issues, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly.
description_plain: Your child takes part in different kinds of group talks on grade 8 topics, listens to others’ ideas, and shares their own clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child writes regularly over short and long periods for different subjects, purposes, and audiences, including time for research and revising.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply grade 8 Reading standards to literary nonfiction (e.g., "Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text [e.g., identifies bias and propaganda techniques, well-supported logical arguments], assessing whether the reasoning is sound and the evidence is relevant and sufficient; recognize when irrelevant evidence is introduced.").
description_plain: Your child can read nonfiction and decide if the arguments and evidence make sense and are relevant.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply grade 8 Reading standards to literature (e.g., "Analyze how a modern work of fiction draws on themes, patterns of events, or character types from myths, traditional stories, or religious works such as the Bible, including describing how the material is rendered new.").
