description: Develop the topic with facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations, or other information/explanations and examples that support the focus.
description_plain: Your child can add facts, definitions, details, or quotes to explain and support the main topic in their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic clearly and group related information in paragraphs and sections; include formatting (e.g., headings), labeled or captioned visuals/diagrams, and multimedia when useful to aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can introduce a topic clearly and group related information into paragraphs, using headings or pictures if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine a topic and convey ideas and information clearly.
description_plain: Your child writes informative texts that explain a topic clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that reinforces or restates the opinion presented.
description_plain: Your child can write an ending that restates their opinion to make it clear and strong.

subject: Language Arts
description: Link opinion and reasons using words and phrases (e.g., for instance, in order to, in addition).
description_plain: Your child can connect their opinion and reasons using words like 'for example' or 'in addition.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide reasons that are supported by facts and details.
description_plain: Your child can give reasons that are backed up with facts and details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic or text clearly, state an opinion, and create an organizational structure in which related ideas are grouped to support the writer's purpose.
description_plain: Your child can clearly introduce a topic, state their opinion, and organize their ideas in paragraphs.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write opinion pieces on topics or texts, supporting a point of view with fact- or text-based reasons and information (e.g., The character ___ was brave because she ____).
description_plain: Your child can write opinion pieces about topics or stories, giving reasons and facts to support their point of view.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, rereading as necessary.
description_plain: Your child can use clues from the sentence to figure out or fix words they don’t know while reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read on-level prose and poetry orally with accuracy, appropriate rate, and expression on successive readings.
description_plain: Your child can read stories and poems out loud with correct words, speed, and expression.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read on-level text with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child can read grade-level texts with understanding and purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read with sufficient accuracy and fluency to support comprehension.
description_plain: Your child reads smoothly and accurately enough to understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use combined knowledge of all letter-sound correspondences, syllabication patterns, and morphology (e.g., roots and affixes) to read accurately unfamiliar multisyllabic words in context and out of context.
description_plain: Your child can use what they know about letters, syllables, and word parts to read long or new words correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words.
description_plain: Your child knows and uses grade-level phonics and word skills to read words.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend a range of informational texts, including history/social studies, science, and technical texts within a complexity band appropriate to grade 4 (from upper grade 3 to grade 5), with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand different kinds of informational texts like history, science, and technical writing at a 4th-grade level.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate information from two texts on the same topic or related topics in order to write or speak about the subject knowledgeably.
description_plain: Your child can use information from two texts about the same or related topics to talk or write about the subject clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how an author uses reasons and evidence to support particular points in a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author uses reasons and evidence to support points in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret information presented visually, orally, or quantitatively (e.g., in charts, graphs, diagrams, time lines, animations, or interactive elements on Web pages) and explain how the information contributes to an understanding of the text in which it appears.
description_plain: Your child can understand and explain information shown in charts, graphs, or pictures and how it helps explain the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine author's purpose; compare and contrast a firsthand and secondhand account of the same event or topic; describe the differences in focus and the information provided.
description_plain: Your child can figure out why an author wrote a text and compare a firsthand and secondhand account of the same event, explaining how they are different.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the overall structure (e.g., sequence, comparison, cause/effect, problem/solution) of events, ideas, concepts, or information in a text or part of a text.
description_plain: Your child can describe how a text is organized, like in order of events, comparing ideas, cause and effect, or problem and solution.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of general academic and domain-specific words or phrases in a text relevant to a grade 4 topic or subject area.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of important words related to grade 4 topics in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain relationships (e.g., cause-effect) among events, procedures, ideas, or concepts in a historical, scientific, or technical text, including what happened and why, based on specific information in the text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how events or ideas are connected in a history, science, or technical text, including what happened and why.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the main idea of a text and explain how it is supported by key details; paraphrase or summarize key ideas, events, or procedures including correct sequence when appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can find the main idea of a text and explain how key details support it, and can retell important ideas or steps in order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Locate explicit information in the text to explain what the text says explicitly and to support inferences drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can find clear information in a text to explain what it says and to support guesses about the meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend a range of literature from a variety of cultures, within a complexity band appropriate to grade 4 (from upper grade 3 to grade 5), with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand different kinds of stories and literature from many cultures at a 4th-grade level.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the treatment of similar themes and plots (e.g., opposition of good and evil) and patterns of events (e.g., the quest) in literature.
description_plain: Your child can compare and talk about similar themes and story patterns, like good vs. evil or quests, in different books.

subject: Language Arts
description: (Not applicable to literature)
description_plain: This standard does not apply to literature.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make connections between the text of a story or drama and a visual or oral presentation of the text, identifying where each version reflects specific descriptions and directions in the text.
description_plain: Your child can connect a story or play to its visual or oral version, pointing out where they match.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the point of view from which different stories are narrated, including how the use of first or third person can change the way a reader might see characters or events described.
description_plain: Your child can compare how different stories are told from different points of view and explain how this changes how we see characters or events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain major differences between poems, drama, and prose, and refer to the structural elements of poems (e.g., verse and rhythm) and drama (e.g., casts of characters, settings, descriptions, dialogue, stage directions) when writing or speaking about a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain the main differences between poems, plays, and stories, and talk about parts like rhythm in poems or dialogue in plays.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including use of figurative language and literary devices (e.g., imagery, metaphors, similes).
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of words and phrases in stories, including when authors use comparisons like similes and metaphors.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe in depth a character, setting, or event in a story or drama, drawing on specific details in the text (e.g., a character's thoughts, words, or actions).
description_plain: Your child can describe a character, setting, or event in detail using specific parts from the story or play.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine a theme or author's message or purpose of a story, drama, or poem using details and evidence from the text as support; summarize main ideas or events, in correct sequence, including how conflicts are resolved.
description_plain: Your child can find the theme or message of a story, drama, or poem using details from the text and can retell the main events in order, including how problems are solved.

subject: Language Arts
description: Refer to details and examples in a text when explaining what the text says explicitly and when drawing inferences from the text.
description_plain: Your child can use details and examples from a text to explain what it says and to make inferences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately a range of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college- and career-readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge when encountering an unknown term important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child can learn and use a wide range of words for reading, writing, speaking, and listening, and can figure out new words on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands figurative language, how words relate to each other, and subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases by using context clues, analyzing meaningful word parts, and consulting general and specialized reference materials, as appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of unknown or tricky words by using clues in the sentence, looking at parts of the word, or checking dictionaries.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child understands how language works in different situations and can choose words and styles that fit what they want to say or understand better when reading or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and word usage when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and communicative tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can change how they speak depending on the situation and use formal English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make strategic use of digital media and visual displays of data to express information and enhance understanding of presentations.
description_plain: Your child can use digital tools and pictures to share information and help others understand their presentations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present information, findings, and supporting evidence such that listeners can follow the line of reasoning and that the organization, development, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can share information and evidence clearly so listeners can follow their ideas and understand the purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate a speaker's point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric.
description_plain: Your child can think about and judge a speaker’s opinions, reasons, and how they use evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child can combine and think about information from different types of media like videos, numbers, and spoken words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Prepare for and participate effectively in a range of conversations and collaborations with diverse partners, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly and persuasively.
description_plain: Your child can prepare for and take part in conversations with different people, building on others’ ideas and sharing their own clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child writes regularly over short and long periods for different reasons and audiences, including time for research and revising.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child can find and use evidence from stories or informational texts to support their thinking and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, assess the credibility and accuracy of each source, and integrate the information while avoiding plagiarism.
description_plain: Your child can gather information from many sources, check if the sources are trustworthy, and use the information without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short as well as more sustained research projects based on focused questions, demonstrating understanding of the subject under investigation.
description_plain: Your child can do short and longer research projects based on specific questions and show they understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child can use technology like the Internet to write, publish their work, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach.
description_plain: Your child can plan, revise, edit, and rewrite their writing to make it better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and organize their ideas in a way that fits the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use narrative writing to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, well-chosen details, and well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or imagined events using good details and clear order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine and convey complex ideas and information clearly and accurately through the effective selection, organization, and analysis of content.
description_plain: Your child can write informative texts that explain complex ideas clearly by choosing and organizing important information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims in an analysis of substantive topics or texts, using valid reasoning and relevant and sufficient evidence.
description_plain: Your child can write arguments that support their opinions with good reasons and evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend a range of complex literary and informational texts independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand difficult stories and informational texts on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can analyze how two or more texts on similar topics build knowledge or show different author approaches.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can find and explain the main argument and claims in a text, checking if the reasons and evidence make sense and are enough.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can combine and think about information from different types of media, like pictures, numbers, and words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child understands how the author's point of view or purpose changes what the text says and how it is written.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of the text (e.g., a section, chapter, scene, or stanza) relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can look at how sentences, paragraphs, and bigger parts of a text fit together and connect to the whole story or article.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text, including determining technical, connotative, and figurative meanings, and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a text, including special or hidden meanings, and explain how word choices affect the feeling or message.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, and ideas grow and connect throughout a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas or themes of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or themes in a text, explain how they develop, and summarize important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what the text says explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child reads carefully to understand what the text says and can make smart guesses, using examples from the text to support their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for reflection and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child can write regularly over short and long times for different school subjects, purposes, and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child uses evidence from informational texts to support their analysis, reflection, and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, using search terms effectively; assess the credibility and accuracy of each source; and quote or paraphrase the data and conclusions of others while avoiding plagiarism and following a standard format for citation.
description_plain: Your child can find useful information from books and online, check if sources are trustworthy, and use others' ideas correctly without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects to answer a question (including a self-generated question), drawing on several sources and generating additional related, focused questions that allow for multiple avenues of exploration.
description_plain: Your child can do short research projects to answer questions, using several sources and asking more focused questions to explore.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and present the relationships between information and ideas clearly and efficiently.
description_plain: Your child can use technology like the Internet to write and share their work clearly and effectively.

subject: Language Arts
description: With some guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach, focusing on how well purpose and audience have been addressed.
description_plain: With some help, your child can improve their writing by planning, revising, editing, or trying new ways to better reach their audience and purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and organized in a way that fits the task, purpose, and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Not applicable as a separate requirement.
description_plain: This standard is not a separate requirement.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the information or explanation presented.
description_plain: Your child can write a conclusion that clearly follows from and supports the information or explanation they gave.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a formal style and objective tone.
description_plain: Your child can write in a formal style and keep an objective tone.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to inform about or explain the topic.
description_plain: Your child can use clear and specific language and special vocabulary to explain a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use appropriate and varied transitions to create cohesion and clarify the relationships among ideas and concepts.
description_plain: Your child can use different transitions to connect ideas and make their writing clear and easy to follow.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with relevant facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations, or other information and examples.
description_plain: Your child can add facts, definitions, details, and quotes to explain a topic clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic clearly, previewing what is to follow; organize ideas, concepts, and information into broader categories as appropriate to achieving purpose; include formatting (e.g., headings) graphics (e.g., charts, tables), and multimedia when useful to aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can clearly introduce a topic, organize ideas into groups, and use headings, charts, or multimedia to help readers understand.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts, including the narration of historical events, scientific procedures/experiments, or technical processes.
description_plain: Your child can write informative texts that explain history events, science experiments, or technical steps.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the argument presented.
description_plain: Your child can write a conclusion that supports and follows from the argument they made.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a formal style.
description_plain: Your child can write in a formal style.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words, phrases, and clauses to create cohesion and clarify the relationships among claim(s), counterclaims, reasons, and evidence.
description_plain: Your child can use words and phrases to connect claims, opposing claims, reasons, and evidence clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Support claim(s) with logical reasoning and relevant, accurate data and evidence that demonstrate an understanding of the topic or text, using credible sources.
description_plain: Your child can support their claims with logical reasons and accurate facts from trustworthy sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce claim(s) abut a topic or issue, acknowledge and distinguish the claim(s) from alternate or opposing claims, and organize the reasons and evidence logically.
description_plain: Your child can introduce their opinion on a topic, recognize other opinions, and organize their reasons and evidence clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments focused on discipline-specific content.
description_plain: Your child can write arguments about topics in specific school subjects.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of grade 8, read and comprehend science/technical texts in the grades 6-8 text complexity band independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand science and technical texts for grades 6 to 8 on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the information gained from experiments, simulations, video, or multimedia sources with that gained from reading a text on the same topic.
description_plain: Your child can compare information from experiments, videos, or simulations with what they read about the same topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish among facts, reasoned judgment based on research findings, and speculation in a text.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between facts, opinions based on research, and guesses in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate quantitative or technical information expressed in words in a text with a version of that information expressed visually (e.g., in a flowchart, diagram, model, graph, or table).
description_plain: Your child can combine information given in words with pictures, charts, or graphs to understand a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analysis the author's purpose in providing an explanation, describing a procedure, or discussing an experiment in a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain why an author gives instructions, describes a process, or talks about an experiment.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure an author uses to organize a text, including how the major sections contribute to the whole and to an understanding of the topic.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author organizes a text and how its parts help explain the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of symbols, key terms, and other domain-specific words and phrases as they are used in a specific scientific or technical context relevant to grades 6-8 texts and topics.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what symbols and special words mean when reading science or technical texts for grades 6–8.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow precisely a multistep procedure when carrying out experiments, taking measurements, or performing technical tasks.
description_plain: Your child can carefully follow multi-step instructions when doing experiments, measuring, or technical tasks.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the central ideas or conclusions of a text; provide an accurate summary of the text distinct from prior knowledge or opinions.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or conclusions in a text and give a clear summary without mixing in their own opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite specific textual evidence to support analysis of science and technical texts.
description_plain: Your child can point to exact parts of a science or technical text to support their understanding and analysis.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of grade 8, read and comprehend history/social studies texts in the grades 6-8 text complexity band independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand history and social studies texts for grades 6-8 on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the relationship between a primary and secondary source on the same topic.
description_plain: Your child can compare and explain how a primary source and a secondary source on the same topic relate to each other.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish among fact, opinion, and reasoned judgment in a text.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between facts, opinions, and thoughtful judgments in what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate visual information (e.g., in charts, graphs, photographs, videos, or maps) with other information in print and digital texts.
description_plain: Your child can use information from pictures, charts, graphs, videos, or maps together with written or digital texts to understand a topic better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify aspects of a text that reveal an author's point of view or purpose (e.g., loaded language, inclusion or avoidance of particular facts).
description_plain: Your child can spot parts of a text that show the author's point of view or purpose, like strong language or what facts are included or left out.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how a text presents information (e.g., sequentially, comparatively, causally)
description_plain: Your child can describe how a text shares information, like in order, by comparing, or showing cause and effect.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including vocabulary specific to domains related to history/social studies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in history or social studies texts, including special vocabulary.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify key steps in a text's description of a process related to history/social studies (e.g., how a bill becomes law, how interest rates are raised or lowered).
description_plain: Your child can identify the important steps in a history or social studies process described in a text, like how a bill becomes a law.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the central ideas or information of a primary or secondary source; provide an accurate summary of the source distinct from prior knowledge or opinions.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or important information in a primary or secondary source and give a clear summary without adding personal opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite specific textual evidence to support analysis of primary and secondary sources.
description_plain: Your child can point to exact parts of primary and secondary sources to support their analysis.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and accurately use grade-appropriate general academic and domain-specific words and phrases; gather vocabulary knowledge when considering a word or phrase important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child can learn and use new academic and subject-specific words correctly to understand and express ideas better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish among the connotations (associations) of words with similar denotations (definitions) (e.g., refined, respectful, polite, diplomatic, condescending).
description_plain: Your child understands the different feelings or ideas that similar words can have, like how 'polite' and 'condescending' mean different things even if they seem related.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the relationship between particular words (e.g., synonym/antonym, analogy) to better understand each of the words.
description_plain: Your child uses relationships between words, like synonyms and antonyms, to understand their meanings better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret the intent or meaning of figures of speech (e.g., literary, religious, and mythological allusions; euphemisms) as used in context.
description_plain: Your child can understand the meaning or purpose of figures of speech like allusions or polite expressions by looking at how they are used.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands figurative language, word relationships, and subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Verify the preliminary determination of the meaning of a word or phrase (e.g., by checking the inferred meaning in context or in a dictionary).
description_plain: Your child checks the meaning of a word or phrase by looking at the sentence or using a dictionary to be sure.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult general and specialized reference materials (e.g., dictionaries, glossaries, and thesauruses), both print and digital, to find the pronunciation of a word or determine or clarify its precise meaning or its part of speech.
description_plain: Your child can use dictionaries, glossaries, or thesauruses, in print or online, to find how to say a word, what it means, or what part of speech it is.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use common, grade-appropriate Greek or Latin affixes and roots as clues to the meaning of a word (e.g., belligerent, bellicose, rebel).
description_plain: Your child uses common Greek or Latin word parts to help figure out what new words mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine meaning of unfamiliar words by using knowledge of word structure, (prefixes/suffixes, base words, common roots, or word origins), context (e.g., the overall meaning of a sentence or paragraph; a word's position or function in a sentence) as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of new words by looking at parts of the word, like prefixes or roots, and by using clues from the sentence or paragraph.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade 7 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out or clarify the meaning of unknown or multiple-meaning words using different strategies.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose language that expresses ideas precisely and concisely, recognizing and eliminating wordiness and redundancy.
description_plain: Your child chooses words that clearly and briefly express ideas, avoiding extra or repeated words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.
description_plain: Your child uses correct language rules when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a comma to separate coordinate adjectives (e.g., It was a fascinating, enjoyable movie.).
description_plain: Your child can use commas correctly to separate two or more adjectives that describe the same noun.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Place phrases and clauses within a sentence, recognizing and correcting misplaced and dangling modifiers.
description_plain: Your child places phrases and clauses correctly in sentences and fixes mistakes when they are confusing or misplaced.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose among simple, compound, complex, and compound-complex sentences to signal differing relationships among ideas.
description_plain: Your child chooses different types of sentences to show how ideas are connected.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the function of phrases and clauses in general and their function in specific sentences in order to apply the conventions of English.
description_plain: Your child can explain what phrases and clauses do in sentences and use this understanding to write correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and language rules when writing or speaking and builds on what they learned before.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate. (See grade 7 Language standards 1 and 3 for specific expectations.)
description_plain: Your child can change how they speak depending on the situation and use formal English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Include multimedia components and visual displays in presentations to clarify claims and findings and emphasize salient points.
description_plain: Your child includes pictures, videos, or other media in presentations to make their points clearer and more interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present claims and findings, emphasizing salient points in a focused, coherent manner with pertinent descriptions, facts, details, and examples; use appropriate eye contact, adequate volume, and clear pronunciation.
description_plain: Your child can present ideas clearly and with important details, using good vocabulary, eye contact, volume, and pronunciation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate a speaker's argument and specific claims, evaluating the soundness of the reasoning and the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can explain a speaker's argument, checking if the reasons and evidence make sense and are enough.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the main ideas and supporting details presented in diverse media and formats (e.g., visually, quantitatively/data-related, orally) and explain how the ideas clarify a topic, text, or issue under study.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas and details in different types of media and explain how they help understand a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acknowledge new information expressed by others and, when warranted, modify their own views.
description_plain: Your child listens to new information from others and changes their own ideas when it makes sense.

subject: Language Arts
description: Pose questions that elicit elaboration and respond to others' questions and comments with relevant observations and ideas that bring the discussion back on topic as needed.
description_plain: Your child asks questions to get more details and responds to others to keep the discussion focused.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow rules for collegial discussions (e.g., establishing norms: taking turns, paraphrasing, respecting diverse viewpoints), track progress toward specific goals and deadlines, and define individual roles as needed.
description_plain: Your child can follow rules for group discussions, like taking turns and respecting others' ideas, keep track of goals and deadlines, and understand their role in the group.

subject: Language Arts
description: Come to discussions prepared, having read or researched material under study; explicitly draw on that preparation by referring to evidence on the topic, text, or issue to probe and reflect on ideas under discussion.
description_plain: Your child comes to discussions prepared by reading or researching and uses evidence to think about ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher-led) with diverse partners on grade 7 topics, texts, and issues, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly.
description_plain: Your child takes part in different kinds of group talks, listens to others, and shares their own ideas clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child writes regularly over short and long periods for different subjects, purposes, and readers.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply grade 7 Reading standards to literary nonfiction (e.g. "Trace and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, assessing whether the reasoning is sound and the evidence is relevant and sufficient to support the claims.").
description_plain: Your child uses reading skills to understand and evaluate arguments and claims in nonfiction texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply grade 7 Reading standards to literature (e.g., "Compare and contrast a fictional portrayal of a time, place, or character and a historical account of the same period as a means of understanding how authors of fiction use or alter history.").
description_plain: Your child compares stories with real history to see how authors change or use facts in fiction.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child uses information from stories or facts to support their thinking, writing, and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, using search terms effectively; assess the credibility and accuracy of each source; and quote or paraphrase the data and conclusions of others while avoiding plagiarism and following a standard format for citation.
description_plain: Your child finds useful information from books and the internet, checks if sources are trustworthy, and uses that information correctly without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects to answer a question, drawing on several sources and generating additional related, focused questions for further research and investigation.
description_plain: Your child does short research projects using several sources and asks new questions to learn more.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and link to and cite sources as well as to interact and collaborate with others, including linking to and citing sources.
description_plain: Your child uses technology to write, share their work, and connect with others, including citing sources properly.

subject: Language Arts
description: With some guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach, focusing on how well purpose and audience have been addressed. (Editing for conventions should demonstrate command of Language standards 1–3 up to and including grade 7.)
description_plain: With some help, your child improves their writing by planning, revising, editing, or trying new ways to better reach their audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience. (Grade-specific expectations for writing types are defined in standards 1–3 above.)
description_plain: Your child writes clearly and in an organized way that fits the task, purpose, and readers.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a conclusion that follows from and reflects on the narrated experiences or events.
description_plain: Your child will write a conclusion that reflects on the experiences or events they described.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise words and phrases, relevant descriptive details, and sensory language to advance the action and convey experiences and events.
description_plain: Your child can use exact words, detailed descriptions, and sensory language to make stories or events clear and interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a variety of transition words, phrases, and clauses to convey sequence and signal shifts from one time frame or setting to another.
description_plain: Your child uses different words and phrases to show the order of events and changes in time or place in stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use narrative techniques, such as dialogue, pacing, and description, to develop experiences, events, and/or characters.
description_plain: Your child uses dialogue, pacing, and descriptions to make stories and characters more interesting and clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage and orient the reader by establishing a context and point of view and introducing a narrator and/or characters; organize an event sequence that unfolds naturally and logically.
description_plain: Your child can start a story by setting the scene, introducing characters or narrator, and organizing events in order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use narrative writing to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, relevant descriptive details, and well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or imagined experiences using good techniques, detailed descriptions, and clear order of events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the information or explanation presented.
description_plain: Your child writes a conclusion that supports the information or explanation they gave.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a formal style.
description_plain: Your child keeps a formal tone in their writing when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to inform about or explain the topic.
description_plain: Your child uses clear and topic-specific words to explain or inform about a subject.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use appropriate transitions to create cohesion and clarify the relationships among ideas and concepts.
description_plain: Your child uses transition words to connect ideas and make their writing easy to follow.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with relevant facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations, or other information and examples.
description_plain: Your child adds facts, definitions, details, quotes, or examples to explain a topic clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic clearly, previewing what is to follow; organize ideas, concepts, and information, using strategies such as definition, classification, comparison/contrast, and cause/effect; include formatting (e.g., headings), graphics (e.g., charts, tables), and multimedia when useful to aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child introduces a topic clearly and organizes information using methods like comparing or explaining causes, and uses headings, charts, or pictures to help readers understand.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine a topic and convey ideas, concepts, and information through the selection, organization, and analysis of relevant content.
description_plain: Your child writes informative texts that explain a topic by choosing and organizing important information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the argument presented.
description_plain: Your child writes a conclusion that supports the argument they made.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a formal style.
description_plain: Your child keeps a formal style when writing arguments.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words, phrases, and clauses to create cohesion and clarify the relationships among claim(s), reasons, and evidence.
description_plain: Your child uses words and phrases to connect ideas and show how claims, reasons, and evidence relate to each other.

subject: Language Arts
description: Support claim(s) with logical reasoning and accurate, relevant evidence, using credible sources and demonstrating an understanding of the topic or text.
description_plain: Your child can support their opinions with clear reasons and accurate evidence from trustworthy sources, showing they understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce claim(s), acknowledge alternate or opposing claims, and organize the reasons and evidence logically.
description_plain: Your child introduces their main points, recognizes other opinions, and organizes reasons and evidence clearly in writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims with clear reasons and relevant evidence.
description_plain: Your child will write arguments that clearly explain their opinions with good reasons and supporting facts.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literary nonfiction, within a complexity band appropriate to grade 7 (from upper grade 6 to grade 8), with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand nonfiction texts at a 7th-grade level, including harder texts with some help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more authors writing about the same topic shape their presentations of key information by emphasizing different evidence or advancing different interpretations of facts.
description_plain: Your child can compare how two or more authors write about the same topic by looking at the different facts or ideas they focus on.

subject: Language Arts
description: Trace and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, assessing whether the reasoning is sound and the evidence is relevant and sufficient to support the claims.
description_plain: Your child can follow and judge the arguments and claims in a text to see if the reasons and evidence really support them.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast a text to an audio, video, or multimedia version of the text, analyzing each medium's portrayal of the subject (e.g., how the method of the delivery of a speech affects the impact of the overall message).
description_plain: Your child can compare a written text to its audio, video, or multimedia version and explain how each way of sharing the story affects its meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine an author's purpose (to inform, persuade, entertain, critique, etc.) and point of view in a text and analyze how the author distinguishes his or her point of view from that of others.
description_plain: Your child can figure out why an author wrote a text and how the author shows their point of view compared to others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure an author uses to organize a text, including how the major sections contribute to the whole and to the development of the ideas.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author organizes a text and how the main sections help develop the ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative, connotative, and technical meanings; analyze the impact of a specific word choice on meaning and tone.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a text, including special or figurative meanings, and explain how word choices affect the tone or meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the interactions between individuals, events, and ideas presented in a text (e.g., how ideas influence individuals or events, or how individuals influence ideas or events).
description_plain: Your child can analyze how people, events, and ideas in a text affect each other.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the central idea and subtopics in a text and analyze their development over the course of the text; restate and summarize the central idea or events, in correct sequence when necessary, after reading a text.
description_plain: Your child can find the main idea and subtopics in a text, explain how they develop, and summarize the main points in order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite several pieces of textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can find several examples from the text to support what it says directly and what can be understood from it.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend a range of literature from a variety of cultures, within a complexity band appropriate to grade 7 (from upper grade 6 to grade 8), with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand literature from many cultures at a 7th-grade level, including harder texts with some help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast a fictional portrayal of a time, place, or character and a historical or cultural account of the same period as a means of understanding how authors of fiction use or alter history.
description_plain: Your child can compare a fictional story about a time or place with a real historical or cultural account to see how authors change history in fiction.

subject: Language Arts
description: (Not applicable to literature)
description_plain: This standard does not apply to literature.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast a written story, drama, or poem to its audio, filmed, staged, or multimedia version, analyzing the effects of techniques unique to each medium (e.g., lighting, sound, color, or camera focus and angles in a film) (e.g., The Incredible Journey, Call of the Wild).
description_plain: Your child can compare a story, play, or poem with its audio, movie, or live version and explain how things like lighting or sound change the experience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze author's purpose and how an author establishes and contrasts the points of view of different characters or narrators in a text.
description_plain: Your child can analyze why an author writes a story and how different characters' or narrators' points of view are shown and compared.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the overall structure of a text: how a drama's or poem's form or structure (e.g. sonnet, soliloquy) contributes to its meaning, including analyzing the impact of rhymes and other repetitions of sounds (e.g., alliteration) on a specific verse or stanza of a poem or section of a story or drama.
description_plain: Your child can analyze how the structure of a poem or drama, like its form or sound patterns, helps show its meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative and connotative meanings; analyze the impact of a specific word choice on meaning and tone.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a story or poem, including special meanings, and explain how word choices affect the feeling or mood.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how particular elements of a story or drama interact (e.g., how setting shapes the characters or plot).
description_plain: Your child can explain how parts of a story or play, like setting and characters, work together.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine a theme or central idea of a text and analyze its development over the course of the text; restate and summarize main ideas or events, in correct sequence, after reading a text.
description_plain: Your child can find the main theme or idea of a story and explain how it develops, then give a clear summary without personal opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite several pieces of textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can find several examples from the story or play to support what it says directly and what can be understood from it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately a range of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college- and career-readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge when encountering an unknown term important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child can learn and use a wide range of words for reading, writing, speaking, and listening, and can figure out new words on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands figurative language, how words relate to each other, and subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases by using context clues, analyzing meaningful word parts, and consulting general and specialized reference materials, as appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of unknown or tricky words by using clues in the sentence, looking at parts of the word, or checking dictionaries.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child understands how language works in different situations and can choose words and styles that fit what they want to say or understand better when reading or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and word usage when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and communicative tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can change how they speak depending on the situation and use formal English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make strategic use of digital media and visual displays of data to express information and enhance understanding of presentations.
description_plain: Your child can use digital tools and pictures to share information and help others understand their presentations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present information, findings, and supporting evidence such that listeners can follow the line of reasoning and that the organization, development, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can share information and evidence clearly so listeners can follow their ideas and understand the purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate a speaker's point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric.
description_plain: Your child can think about and judge a speaker’s opinions, reasons, and how they use evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child can combine and think about information from different types of media like videos, numbers, and spoken words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Prepare for and participate effectively in a range of conversations and collaborations with diverse partners, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly and persuasively.
description_plain: Your child can prepare for and take part in conversations with different people, building on others’ ideas and sharing their own clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child writes regularly over short and long periods for different reasons and audiences, including time for research and revising.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child can find and use evidence from stories or informational texts to support their thinking and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, assess the credibility and accuracy of each source, and integrate the information while avoiding plagiarism.
description_plain: Your child can gather information from many sources, check if the sources are trustworthy, and use the information without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short as well as more sustained research projects based on focused questions, demonstrating understanding of the subject under investigation.
description_plain: Your child can do short and longer research projects based on specific questions and show they understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child can use technology like the Internet to write, publish their work, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach.
description_plain: Your child can plan, revise, edit, and rewrite their writing to make it better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and organize their ideas in a way that fits the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use narrative writing to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, well-chosen details, and well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or imagined events using good details and clear order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine and convey complex ideas and information clearly and accurately through the effective selection, organization, and analysis of content.
description_plain: Your child can write informative texts that explain complex ideas clearly by choosing and organizing important information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims in an analysis of substantive topics or texts, using valid reasoning and relevant and sufficient evidence.
description_plain: Your child can write arguments that support their opinions with good reasons and evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend a range of complex literary and informational texts independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand difficult stories and informational texts on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can analyze how two or more texts on similar topics build knowledge or show different author approaches.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can find and explain the main argument and claims in a text, checking if the reasons and evidence make sense and are enough.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can combine and think about information from different types of media, like pictures, numbers, and words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child understands how the author's point of view or purpose changes what the text says and how it is written.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of the text (e.g., a section, chapter, scene, or stanza) relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can look at how sentences, paragraphs, and bigger parts of a text fit together and connect to the whole story or article.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text, including determining technical, connotative, and figurative meanings, and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a text, including special or hidden meanings, and explain how word choices affect the feeling or message.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, and ideas grow and connect throughout a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas or themes of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or themes in a text, explain how they develop, and summarize important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what the text says explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child reads carefully to understand what the text says and can make smart guesses, using examples from the text to support their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for reflection and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child will write regularly over short and long periods for different subjects, purposes, and audiences, allowing time to think and improve.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for reflection and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child will write regularly over short and long periods for different subjects, purposes, and audiences, allowing time to think and improve.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child can find and use evidence from informational texts to support thinking, reflection, and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child can find and use evidence from informational texts to support thinking, reflection, and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple authoritative print and digital sources, using advanced searches effectively; assess the usefulness of each source in answering the research question; integrate information into the text selectively to maintain the flow of ideas, avoiding plagiarism and following a standard format for citation.
description_plain: Your child can gather useful information from trusted print and online sources, decide which is best for their question, and use it properly without copying.

subject: Language Arts

description_plain: Your child can gather useful information from trusted print and online sources, decide which is best for their question, and use it properly without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short as well as more sustained research projects to answer a question (including a self-generated question) or solve a problem; narrow or broaden the inquiry when appropriate; synthesize multiple sources on the subject, demonstrating understanding of the subject under investigation.
description_plain: Your child can do short or longer research projects to answer questions or solve problems, adjusting the focus as needed and combining information from many sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short as well as more sustained research projects to answer a question (including a self-generated question) or solve a problem; narrow or broaden the inquiry when appropriate; synthesize multiple sources on the subject, demonstrating understanding of the subject under investigation.
description_plain: Your child can do short or longer research projects to answer questions or solve problems, adjusting the focus as needed and combining information from many sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce, publish, and update individual or shared writing products, taking advantage of technology's capacity to link to other information and to display information flexibly and dynamically.
description_plain: Your child can use technology like the Internet to create, share, and update writing, including linking to other information and showing ideas clearly.

description_plain: Your child can use technology like the Internet to create, share, and update writing, including linking to other information and showing ideas clearly.

description_plain: Your child can improve their writing by planning, revising, editing, or trying new ways to focus on what matters most for their audience and purpose.

description: Compare and contrast findings presented in a text to those from other sources (including their own experiments), noting when the findings support or contradict previous explanations or accounts.

description_plain: Your child can write clearly and logically, using the right style and organization for the type of writing and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a conclusion that follows from and reflects on what is experienced, observed, or resolved over the course of the narrative.

description: Provide a conclusion that follows from and reflects on what is experienced, observed, or resolved over the course of the narrative.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise words and phrases, telling details, and sensory language to convey a vivid picture of the experiences, events, setting, and/or characters.

subject: Language Arts
description:
By the end of grade 9, read and comprehend literary nonfiction, within a complexity band appropriate to grade 9 (from upper grade 8 to grade 10), with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
By the end of grade 10, read and comprehend literary nonfiction, within a complexity band appropriate to grade 10 (from upper grade 9 to grade 11), with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand nonfiction books and articles that are appropriate for their grade, with help if needed for harder texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze seminal U.S. and world documents of historical and literary significance (e.g., Washington's Farewell Address, the Gettysburg Address, Roosevelt's Four Freedoms speech, King's "Letter from Birmingham Jail"), including how they address related themes and concepts.

subject: Language Arts
