description_plain: Your child can learn and use academic and subject-specific words correctly for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at a college level, and can find out new word meanings on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze nuances in the meaning of words with similar denotations (definition) or determine the meaning of analogies.
description_plain: Your child can understand subtle differences between similar words and figure out the meaning of analogies.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze nuances in the meaning of words with similar denotations (definition) or determine the meaning of analogies.
description_plain: Your child can understand subtle differences between similar words and figure out the meaning of analogies.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret figures of speech (e.g., hyperbole, paradox) in context and analyze their role in the text.
description_plain: Your child can understand and explain figures of speech like exaggerations or contradictions in what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret figures of speech (e.g., hyperbole, paradox) in context and analyze their role in the text.
description_plain: Your child can understand and explain figures of speech like exaggerations or contradictions in what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands figurative language, word relationships, and subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands figurative language, word relationships, and subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Verify the preliminary determination of the meaning of a word or phrase (e.g., by checking the inferred meaning in context or in a dictionary).
description_plain: Your child can check the meaning of a word or phrase by looking at the context or using a dictionary.

subject: Language Arts
description: Verify the preliminary determination of the meaning of a word or phrase (e.g., by checking the inferred meaning in context or in a dictionary).
description_plain: Your child can check the meaning of a word or phrase by looking at the context or using a dictionary.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult general and specialized reference materials (e.g., dictionaries, glossaries, thesauruses), both print and digital, to find the pronunciation of a word or determine or clarify its precise meaning, its part of speech, its etymology, or its standard usage.
description_plain: Your child can use dictionaries and other resources to find how to say a word, what it means, and how to use it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult general and specialized reference materials (e.g., dictionaries, glossaries, thesauruses), both print and digital, to find the pronunciation of a word or determine or clarify its precise meaning, its part of speech, its etymology, or its standard usage.
description_plain: Your child can use dictionaries and other resources to find how to say a word, what it means, and how to use it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify and correctly use patterns of word changes that indicate different meanings or parts of speech (e.g., conceive, conception, conceivable).
description_plain: Your child can recognize and use word parts that change a word’s meaning or type, like 'conceive' and 'conception.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify and correctly use patterns of word changes that indicate different meanings or parts of speech (e.g., conceive, conception, conceivable).
description_plain: Your child can recognize and use word parts that change a word’s meaning or type, like 'conceive' and 'conception.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine meanings of unfamiliar words by using knowledge of derivational roots and affixes, including cultural derivations (e.g., the root of photography and photosynthesis; kayak), context (e.g., the overall meaning of a sentence, paragraph, or text; a word's position or function in a sentence), dialectical English (e.g., Huck Finn), idiomatic expressions (e.g., "it drives me up a wall") as clues to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of new words by using knowledge of word parts, cultural origins, sentence clues, dialects, and common expressions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine meanings of unfamiliar words by using knowledge of derivational roots and affixes, including cultural derivations (e.g., the root of photography and photosynthesis; kayak), context (e.g., the overall meaning of a sentence, paragraph, or text; a word's position or function in a sentence), dialectical English (e.g., Huck Finn), idiomatic expressions (e.g., "it drives me up a wall") as clues to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of new words by using knowledge of word parts, cultural origins, sentence clues, dialects, and common expressions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grades 11-12 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out or clarify the meaning of unknown or multiple-meaning words using different strategies.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grades 11-12 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out or clarify the meaning of unknown or multiple-meaning words using different strategies.

subject: Language Arts
description: Vary syntax for effect, consulting references (e.g., Tufte's Artful Sentences) for guidance as needed; apply an understanding of syntax to the study of complex texts when reading.
description_plain: Your child can change sentence structure to create effects and understands complex sentences when reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Vary syntax for effect, consulting references (e.g., Tufte's Artful Sentences) for guidance as needed; apply an understanding of syntax to the study of complex texts when reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child understands how language works in different situations and can choose words and styles to express meaning clearly when reading, writing, or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Observe hyphenation conventions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Observe hyphenation conventions.
description_plain: Your child knows and uses correct hyphenation rules when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Resolve issues of complex or contested usage, consulting references (e.g., Merriam-Webster's Dictionary of English Usage, Garner's Modern American Usage) as needed.
description_plain: Your child can solve tricky grammar or usage questions by checking trusted references.

subject: Language Arts
description: Resolve issues of complex or contested usage, consulting references (e.g., Merriam-Webster's Dictionary of English Usage, Garner's Modern American Usage) as needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply the understanding that usage is a matter of convention, can change over time, and is sometimes contested.

description: Apply the understanding that usage is a matter of convention, can change over time, and is sometimes contested.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child can use correct grammar and language rules when writing or speaking and build on what they learned before.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and tasks, demonstrating a command of formal English when indicated or appropriate and addressing intended audience needs and knowledge level. (See grades 11–12 Language standards 1 and 3 for specific expectations.)
description_plain: Your child can change how they speak depending on the situation and use formal English when it is needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and tasks, demonstrating a command of formal English when indicated or appropriate and addressing intended audience needs and knowledge level. (See grades 11–12 Language standards 1 and 3 for specific expectations.)
description_plain: Your child can change how they speak depending on the situation and use formal English when it is needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make strategic use of digital media (e.g., textual, graphical, audio, visual, and interactive elements) in presentations to enhance understanding of findings, reasoning, and evidence and to add interest.
description_plain: Your child can use digital tools like pictures, sounds, and videos in presentations to help others understand their ideas and keep them interested.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make strategic use of digital media (e.g., textual, graphical, audio, visual, and interactive elements) in presentations to enhance understanding of findings, reasoning, and evidence and to add interest.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present information, findings, and supporting evidence, conveying a clear and distinct perspective, such that listeners can follow the line of reasoning, alternative or opposing perspectives are addressed, and the organization, development, substance, and style are appropriate to purpose, audience, and a range or formal and informal tasks.
description_plain: Your child can clearly share information and ideas in presentations, explain their thinking, address other views, and organize their talk for different audiences and settings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present information, findings, and supporting evidence, conveying a clear and distinct perspective, such that listeners can follow the line of reasoning, alternative or opposing perspectives are addressed, and the organization, development, substance, and style are appropriate to purpose, audience, and a range or formal and informal tasks.
description_plain: Your child can clearly share information and ideas in presentations, explain their thinking, address other views, and organize their talk for different audiences and settings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify and evaluate a speaker's point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric, assessing the stance, premises, links among ideas, word choice, points of emphasis, and tone used.
description_plain: Your child can understand and evaluate a speaker’s opinion, reasons, evidence, word choices, and tone.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify and evaluate a speaker's point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric, assessing the stance, premises, links among ideas, word choice, points of emphasis, and tone used.
description_plain: Your child can understand and evaluate a speaker’s opinion, reasons, evidence, word choices, and tone.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate multiple sources of information presented in diverse formats and media (e.g., visually, quantitatively, orally) in order to make informed decisions and solve problems, evaluating the credibility and accuracy of each source and noting any discrepancies among the data or information.
description_plain: Your child can combine information from different sources like charts, videos, and talks to make smart decisions and solve problems, checking if the information is trustworthy.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate multiple sources of information presented in diverse formats and media (e.g., visually, quantitatively, orally) in order to make informed decisions and solve problems, evaluating the credibility and accuracy of each source and noting any discrepancies among the data or information.
description_plain: Your child can combine information from different sources like charts, videos, and talks to make smart decisions and solve problems, checking if the information is trustworthy.

subject: Language Arts
description: Respond thoughtfully to diverse perspectives or arguments; synthesize comments, claims, and evidence made on all sides of an issue; resolve contradictions when possible; and determine what additional information or research is required to deepen the investigation or complete the task.
description_plain: Your child can respond thoughtfully to different opinions, combine ideas from all sides, resolve conflicts when possible, and decide what more information is needed.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
description: Work with peers to promote civil, democratic discussions and decision-making, set clear goals and deadlines, and establish individual roles as needed.

description: Work with peers to promote civil, democratic discussions and decision-making, set clear goals and deadlines, and establish individual roles as needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Come to discussions prepared, having read and researched material under study; explicitly draw on that preparation by referring to evidence from texts and other research on the topic or issue to stimulate a thoughtful, well-reasoned exchange of ideas.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply grades 11–12 Reading standards to literary nonfiction (e.g., "Delineate and evaluate the reasoning in seminal U.S. texts, including the application of constitutional principles and use of legal reasoning [e.g., in U.S. Supreme Court Case majority opinions and dissents] and the premises, purposes, and arguments in works of public advocacy [e.g., The Federalist, presidential addresses].").
description_plain: Your child can read and understand important U.S. nonfiction texts, like Supreme Court opinions and speeches, and explain the reasoning and arguments they contain.

description_plain: Your child can read and understand important U.S. nonfiction texts, like Supreme Court opinions and speeches, and explain the reasoning and arguments they contain.

subject: Language Arts
description:
By the end of grade 11, read and comprehend literary nonfiction, within a complexity band appropriate to grade 11 (from upper grade 10 to grade 12), with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
By the end of grade 12, read and comprehend literary nonfiction at the high end of the grades 11–12 text complexity band independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand challenging nonfiction books and articles independently by the end of grade 12.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze seventeenth-, eighteenth-, and nineteenth-century foundational U.S. and world documents of historical and literary significance (including The Declaration of Independence, the Preamble to the Constitution, the Bill of Rights, and Lincoln's Second Inaugural Address) for their themes, purposes, and rhetorical features.
description_plain: Your child can study important historical U.S. and world documents and explain their themes, purposes, and writing styles.

subject: Language Arts

description_plain: Your child can look at and compare flat and solid shapes of different sizes and positions, and talk about how they are alike or different, including parts like sides and corners.

subject: Math
description: Identify shapes as two-dimensional (flat) or three-dimensional (solid).
description_plain: Your child can tell if a shape is flat (2D) or solid (3D).

subject: Math
description: Name shapes regardless of their orientation or overall size.
description_plain: Your child can name shapes no matter how they are turned or how big they are.

subject: Math
description: Describe objects in the environment using names of shapes and describe their relative positions (e.g., above, below, beside, in front of, behind, next to).
description_plain: Your child can describe things around them using shape names and words about where things are, like above, below, or next to.

subject: Math
description: Tell time to the hour using both analog and digital clocks.
description_plain: Your child can tell time to the hour using both clocks with hands and digital clocks.

subject: Math
description: Name in sequence the days of the week.
description_plain: Your child can say the days of the week in order.

subject: Math
description: Classify objects into given categories (attributes). Count the number of objects in each category (limit category counts to be less than or equal to 10).
description_plain: Your child can sort objects into groups based on their features and count how many are in each group, up to 10.

subject: Math
description: Make comparisons between two objects with a measurable attribute in common, to see which object has "more of"/"less of" the attribute, and describe the difference.
description_plain: Your child can compare two objects to see which has more or less of something measurable and describe the difference.

subject: Math
description: Describe measurable attributes of objects (e.g., length or weight). Match measuring tools to attribute (e.g., ruler to length). Describe several measurable attributes of a single object.
description_plain: Your child can describe things you can measure, like length or weight, pick the right tool to measure, and talk about different measurable features of one object.

subject: Math
description: Compose and decompose numbers from 11 to 19 into ten ones and some further ones (e.g., by using objects or drawings) and record each composition and decomposition by a drawing or equation (e.g., 18 = 10 + 8); understand that these numbers are composed of ten ones and one, two, three, four, five, six, seven, eight or nine ones.
description_plain: Your child can break apart numbers from 11 to 19 into ten and some more ones using objects or drawings and write this as a drawing or equation.

subject: Math
description: Recognize, identify and continue simple patterns of color, shape, and size.
description_plain: Your child can recognize, name, and continue simple patterns with colors, shapes, and sizes.

subject: Math
description: Fluently add and subtract numbers up to 5.
description_plain: Your child can quickly add and subtract numbers up to 5.

subject: Math
description: For any number from 1 - 4, find the number that makes 5 when added to the given number and, for any number from 1 - 9, find the number that makes 10 when added to the given number (e.g., by using objects, drawings or 10 frames) and record the answer with a drawing or equation.
description_plain: Your child can find the number that adds to a given number to make 5 or 10 using objects or drawings and write the answer as a drawing or equation.

subject: Math
description: Decompose numbers less than or equal to 10 into pairs in more than one way (e.g., by using objects or drawings, and record each decomposition by a drawing or equation).
description_plain: Your child can break apart numbers up to 10 in different ways using objects or drawings and show each way with pictures or simple equations.

subject: Math
description: Add or subtract whole numbers to 10 (e.g., by using objects or drawings to solve word problems).
description_plain: Your child can add and subtract numbers up to 10 by using objects or drawings to solve simple story problems.

subject: Math
description: Represent addition and subtraction with objects, fingers, mental images, drawings, sounds (e.g., claps) acting out situations, verbal explanations, expressions, or equations.
description_plain: Your child can show addition and subtraction using things like objects, fingers, drawings, sounds, acting, or words.

subject: Math
description: Compare and order two numbers between 1 and 10 presented as written numerals.
description_plain: Your child can look at two numbers from 1 to 10 and say which is bigger, smaller, or if they are the same.

subject: Math
description: Identify whether the number of objects in one group is greater than, less than, or equal to the number of objects in another group (e.g., by using matching, counting, or estimating strategies).
description_plain: Your child can tell if one group of up to 10 objects has more, less, or the same number as another group.

subject: Math
description: Count to answer "how many?" questions about as many as 20 things arranged in a line, a rectangular array or a circle, or as many as 10 things in a scattered configuration; given a number from 1-20, count out that many objects.
description_plain: Your child can count up to 20 things in a line, rectangle, or circle, and count up to 10 things that are scattered; they can also count out a specific number of objects from 1 to 20.

subject: Math
description: Understand that each successive number name refers to a quantity that is one larger.
description_plain: Your child understands that when you say numbers in order, each number is one more than the one before.

subject: Math
description: Understand that the last number name said tells the number of objects counted. The number of objects is the same regardless of their arrangement or the order in which they were counted.
description_plain: Your child understands that the last number said when counting tells how many objects there are, no matter how they are arranged or counted.

subject: Math
description: When counting objects, say the number names in standard order, pairing each object with one and only one number name and each number name with one and only one object.
description_plain: Your child can count objects by saying number names in order, matching one number to each object and one object to each number.

subject: Math
description: Understand the relationship between numbers and quantities; connect counting to cardinality.
description_plain: Your child understands how numbers relate to amounts and connects counting to knowing how many things there are.

subject: Math
description: Write numbers from 0 to 20. Represent a number of objects with a written numeral 0 - 20 (with 0 representing a count of no objects).
description_plain: Your child can write numbers from 0 to 20 and match written numbers to the right number of objects, including zero for no objects.

subject: Math
description: Count forward beginning from a given number within the known sequence.
description_plain: Your child can start counting forward from any number they already know.

subject: Math
description: Count to 100 by ones and by tens.
description_plain: Your child can count to 100 by ones and by tens.

subject: Math
description: Look for and express regularity in repeated reasoning.
description_plain: Your child can notice and explain patterns or repeated steps when solving problems.

subject: Math
description: Look for and make use of structure.
description_plain: Your child can notice and use the way math problems are put together to help solve them.

subject: Math
description: Use appropriate tools strategically.
description_plain: Your child can choose and use the right tools, like calculators, rulers, or graphs, to help solve math problems.

subject: Math
description: Construct viable arguments and critique the reasoning of others.
description_plain: Your child can explain their math thinking and listen to and question the ideas of others.

subject: Math
description: Reason abstractly and quantitatively.
description_plain: Your child can think about math problems in different ways, using numbers and real-life examples.

subject: Math
description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them.
description_plain: Your child can understand what a math problem is asking and keep working until they find a solution.

subject: Science
description: Analyze and discuss data from tests of two objects designed to solve the same problem to compare the strengths and weaknesses of how each performs.
description_plain: Your child can look at test results from two things made to solve the same problem and talk about what each does well and not so well.

subject: Science
description: Develop a simple sketch, drawing, or physical model to illustrate how the shape of an object helps it function as needed to solve a given problem.
description_plain: Your child can draw or build a simple model to show how the shape of something helps it do its job.

subject: Science
description: Ask questions, make observations, and gather information about a situation people want to change to define a simple problem that can be solved through the development of a new or improved object or tool.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions and gather information to understand a problem that people want to fix by making a new or better tool.

subject: Science
description: Ask questions to obtain information about the purpose of weather forecasting to prepare for, and respond to, severe weather.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions to learn why weather forecasts help people get ready for bad weather.

subject: Science
description: Use and share observations of local weather conditions to describe patterns over time.
description_plain: Your child will watch and share what the weather is like in your area to notice patterns over time.

subject: Science
description: Use tools and materials to design and build a structure that will reduce the warming effect of sunlight on an area.
description_plain: Your child can use tools and materials to build something that helps keep an area cooler by blocking sunlight.

subject: Science
description: Make observations to determine the effect of sunlight on Earth's surface.
description_plain: Your child will observe and describe how sunlight affects the Earth's surface.

subject: Science
description: Communicate solutions that will reduce the impact of humans on the land, water, air, and/or other living things in the local environment.
description_plain: Your child can share ideas to help reduce how people harm the land, water, air, or living things nearby.

subject: Science
description: Use a model to represent the relationship between the needs of different plants and animals (including humans) and the places they live.
description_plain: Your child can use a model to show how different plants and animals, including people, need certain things and live in certain places.

subject: Science
description: Construct an argument supported by evidence for how plants and animals (including humans) can change the environment to meet their needs.
description_plain: Your child can explain with examples how plants and animals (including people) change their surroundings to meet their needs.

subject: Science
description: Use observations to describe patterns of what plants and animals (including humans) need to survive.
description_plain: Your child will watch and describe what plants and animals (including people) need to live and grow.

subject: Science
description: Analyze data to determine if a design solution works as intended to change the speed or direction of an object with a push or a pull.
description_plain: Your child can look at data to decide if a design works to change how fast or which way an object moves when pushed or pulled.

subject: Science
description: Plan and conduct an investigation to compare the effects of different strengths or different directions of pushes and pulls on the motion of an object.
description_plain: Your child will plan and do an experiment to see how different pushes and pulls affect how an object moves.

subject: Science
description: Analyze and discuss data from tests of two objects designed to solve the same problem to compare the strengths and weaknesses of how each performs.
description_plain: Your child can look at test results from two things made to solve the same problem and talk about what each does well and not so well.

subject: Science
description: Develop a simple sketch, drawing, or physical model to illustrate how the shape of an object helps it function as needed to solve a given problem.
description_plain: Your child can draw or build a simple model to show how the shape of something helps it do its job.

subject: Science
description: Ask questions, make observations, and gather information about a situation people want to change to define a simple problem that can be solved through the development of a new or improved object or tool.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions and gather information to understand a problem that people want to fix by making a new or better tool.

subject: Science
description: Make and graph observations at different times of year to relate the amount of daylight to the time of year, and graph findings.
description_plain: Your child can watch and record how daylight changes during the year and make a graph to show what they find.

subject: Science
description: Use observations of the sun, moon, stars, and tides to describe patterns that can be predicted.
description_plain: Your child can use what they see about the sun, moon, stars, and tides to describe patterns that happen again and again.

subject: Science
description: Make observations to construct an evidence-based account that young plants and animals are like, but not exactly like, their parents.
description_plain: Your child can observe young plants and animals and explain how they are similar but not exactly like their parents.

subject: Science
description: Read texts and use media to determine patterns in behavior of parents and offspring that help offspring survive.
description_plain: Your child can read and watch videos to learn how parents and their babies behave in ways that help the babies survive.

subject: Science
description: Use materials to design a solution to a human problem by mimicking how plants and/or animals use their external parts to help them survive, grow, and meet their needs.
description_plain: Your child can use materials to create a solution to a problem by copying how plants or animals use their body parts to survive and grow.

subject: Science
description: Use tools and materials to design and build a device that uses light or sound to solve the problem of communicating over a distance.
description_plain: Your child can design and build a tool that uses light or sound to help people communicate from far away.

subject: Science
description: Plan and conduct investigations to determine the effect of placing objects made with different materials in the path of a beam of light.
description_plain: Your child can plan and do simple tests to see what happens when different materials block a beam of light.

subject: Science
description: Make observations to construct an evidence-based account that objects in darkness can be seen only when illuminated.
description_plain: Your child can observe and explain that things in the dark can only be seen when light shines on them.

subject: Science
description: Plan and conduct investigations to provide evidence that vibrating materials can make sound and that sound can make materials vibrate.
description_plain: Your child can plan and do experiments to show that vibrating things make sound and sound can make things vibrate.

subject: Science
description: Analyze and discuss data from tests of two objects designed to solve the same problem to compare the strengths and weaknesses of how each performs.
description_plain: Your child can look at test results from two things made to solve the same problem and talk about what each does well and not so well.

subject: Science
description: Develop a simple sketch, drawing, or physical model to illustrate how the shape of an object helps it function as needed to solve a given problem.
description_plain: Your child can draw or build a simple model to show how the shape of something helps it do its job.

subject: Science
description: Ask questions, make observations, and gather information about a situation people want to change to define a simple problem that can be solved through the development of a new or improved object or tool.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions and gather information to understand a problem that people want to fix by making a new or better tool.

subject: Science
description: Obtain information to identify where water is found on Earth and that it can be solid or liquid.
description_plain: Your child can find out where water is on Earth and understand that water can be ice or liquid.

subject: Science
description: Develop a model to represent the shapes and kinds of land and bodies of water in an area.
description_plain: Your child can create a model that shows the shapes and types of land and water in a certain place.

subject: Science
description: Compare multiple solutions designed to slow or prevent wind or water from changing the shape of the land.
description_plain: Your child can compare different ways people try to stop wind or water from changing the land’s shape.

subject: Science
description: Use information from several sources to provide evidence that Earth events can occur quickly or slowly.
description_plain: Your child can use information from different places to show that Earth changes can happen fast or slowly.

subject: Science
description: Make observations of plants and animals to compare the diversity of life in different habitats.
description_plain: Your child can watch plants and animals and compare how many different kinds live in different places.

subject: Science
description: Develop a simple model that mimics the function of an animal in dispersing seeds or pollinating plants.
description_plain: Your child can make a simple model that shows how an animal helps spread seeds or pollinate plants.

subject: Science
description: Plan and conduct an investigation to determine if plants need sunlight and water to grow.
description_plain: Your child can plan and do an experiment to see if plants need sunlight and water to grow.

subject: Science
description: Construct an argument with evidence that some changes caused by heating or cooling can be reversed and some cannot.
description_plain: Your child can explain with evidence which changes caused by heating or cooling can be undone and which cannot.

subject: Science
description: Make observations to construct an evidence-based account of how an object made of a small set of pieces can be disassembled and made into a new object.
description_plain: Your child can watch and describe how an object made of a few parts can be taken apart and made into something new.

subject: Science
description: Analyze data obtained from testing different materials to determine which materials have the properties that are best suited for an intended purpose.
description_plain: Your child can test different materials and decide which ones work best for a certain use.

subject: Science
description: Plan and conduct an investigation to describe and classify different kinds of materials by their observable properties.
description_plain: Your child can plan and do an experiment to describe and sort materials by what they look and feel like.

subject: Science
description: Plan and carry out fair tests in which variables are controlled and failure points are considered to identify aspects of a model or prototype that can be improved.
description_plain: Your child can plan and do fair tests by controlling variables and thinking about what might go wrong to find ways to improve a model or prototype.

subject: Science
description: Generate and compare multiple possible solutions to a problem based on how well each is likely to meet the criteria and constraints of the problem.
description_plain: Your child can come up with and compare several possible solutions to a problem to see which ones best meet the goals and limits.

subject: Science
description: Define a simple design problem reflecting a need or a want that includes specified criteria for success and constraints on materials, time, or cost.
description_plain: Your child can describe a simple design problem based on a need or want, including what success looks like and limits like materials, time, or cost.

subject: Science
description: Make a claim about the merit of a design solution that reduces the impacts of a weather-related hazard.
description_plain: Your child can explain why a design helps reduce problems caused by bad weather.

subject: Science
description: Obtain and combine information to describe climates in different regions of the world.
description_plain: Your child will gather and combine information to describe different climates around the world.

subject: Science
description: Represent data in tables and graphical displays to describe typical weather conditions expected during a particular season.
description_plain: Your child can show weather data in tables and graphs to explain what weather is usually like in a season.

subject: Science
description: Use evidence to construct an explanation for how the variations in characteristics among individuals of the same species may provide advantages in surviving, finding mates, and reproducing.
description_plain: Your child understands how differences in animals or plants help them survive, find mates, and have babies.

subject: Science
description: Use evidence to support the explanation that traits can be influenced by the environment.
description_plain: Your child can use evidence to show how the environment can change traits in living things.

subject: Science
description: Analyze and interpret data to provide evidence that plants and animals have traits inherited from parents and that variation of these traits exists in a group of similar organisms.
description_plain: Your child can look at data to show that plants and animals get traits from their parents and that these traits can vary.

subject: Science
description: Develop models to describe that organisms have unique and diverse life cycles but all have in common birth, growth, reproduction, and death.
description_plain: Your child can create models to show that all living things have life stages like birth, growth, reproduction, and death.

subject: Science
description: Make a claim about the merit of a solution to a problem caused when the environment changes and the types of plants and animals that live there may change.
description_plain: Your child can explain if a solution is good for helping plants and animals when their environment changes.

subject: Science
description: Construct an argument with evidence that in a particular habitat some organisms can survive well, some survive less well, and some cannot survive at all.
description_plain: Your child can explain with evidence why some animals live well in a habitat, some live less well, and some cannot live there at all.

subject: Science
description: Analyze and interpret data from fossils to provide evidence of the organisms and the environments in which they lived long ago.
description_plain: Your child can study fossils to learn about animals and environments from long ago.

subject: Science
description: Construct an argument that some animals form groups that help members survive.
description_plain: Your child can explain why some animals live in groups to help each other survive.

subject: Science
description: Define a simple design problem that can be solved by applying scientific ideas about magnets.
description_plain: Your child can describe a simple problem that can be solved using ideas about magnets.

subject: Science
description: Ask questions to determine cause and effect relationships of electric or magnetic interactions between two objects not in contact with each other.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions to find out how electric or magnetic forces affect objects without touching.

subject: Science
description: Make observations and/or measurements of an object's motion to provide evidence that a pattern can be used to predict future motion.
description_plain: Your child can watch and measure how things move to find patterns that help predict future motion.

subject: Science
description: Plan and conduct an investigation to provide evidence of the effects of balanced and unbalanced forces on the motion of an object.
description_plain: Your child can plan and do an experiment to show how balanced and unbalanced forces change how things move.

subject: Science
description: Plan and carry out fair tests in which variables are controlled and failure points are considered to identify aspects of a model or prototype that can be improved.
description_plain: Your child can plan and do fair tests by controlling variables and thinking about what might go wrong to find ways to improve a model or prototype.

subject: Science
description: Generate and compare multiple possible solutions to a problem based on how well each is likely to meet the criteria and constraints of the problem.
description_plain: Your child can come up with and compare several possible solutions to a problem to see which ones best meet the goals and limits.

subject: Science
description: Define a simple design problem reflecting a need or a want that includes specified criteria for success and constraints on materials, time, or cost.
description_plain: Your child can describe a simple design problem based on a need or want, including what success looks like and limits like materials, time, or cost.

subject: Science
description: Generate and compare multiple solutions to reduce the impacts of natural Earth processes on humans.
description_plain: Your child comes up with and compares different ideas to lessen how natural Earth events impact people.

subject: Science
description: Analyze and interpret data from maps to describe patterns of Earth's features.
description_plain: Your child looks at maps and data to describe patterns in Earth’s features.

subject: Science
description: Make observations and/or measurements to provide evidence of the effects of weathering or the rate of erosion by water, ice, wind, or vegetation.
description_plain: Your child observes or measures how weathering or erosion by water, ice, wind, or plants changes the land.

subject: Science
description: Identify evidence from patterns in rock formations and fossils in rock layers to support an explanation for changes in a landscape over time.
description_plain: Your child uses patterns in rocks and fossils to explain how a landscape has changed over time.

subject: Science
description: Use a model to describe that animals receive different types of information through their senses, process the information in their brain, and respond to the information in different ways.
description_plain: Your child uses a model to show how animals get information through their senses, process it in their brain, and respond in different ways.

subject: Science
description: Construct an argument that plants and animals have internal and external structures that function to support survival, growth, behavior, and reproduction.
description_plain: Your child explains how plants and animals have body parts inside and outside that help them survive, grow, behave, and reproduce.

subject: Science
description: Develop a model to describe that light reflecting from objects and entering the eye allows objects to be seen.
description_plain: Your child makes a model to show that we see objects because light reflects off them and enters our eyes.

subject: Science
description: Generate and compare multiple solutions that use patterns to transfer information.
description_plain: Your child thinks of and compares different ways patterns can be used to send information.

subject: Science
description: Develop and use a model of waves to describe patterns in terms of amplitude and wavelength and that waves can cause objects to move.
description_plain: Your child can make and use a model to show how waves have height and length and how waves can make things move.

subject: Science
description: Obtain and combine information to describe that energy and fuels are derived from natural resources and their uses affect the environment.
description_plain: Your child will gather information to explain that energy and fuels come from natural resources and that using them can affect the environment.

subject: Science
description: Apply scientific ideas to design, test, and refine a device that converts energy from one form to another.
description_plain: Your child can design, test, and improve a device that changes energy from one type to another.

subject: Science
description: Ask questions and predict outcomes about the changes in energy that occur when objects collide.
description_plain: Your child will ask questions and guess what will happen to energy when objects bump into each other.

subject: Science
description: Make observations to provide evidence that energy can be transferred from place to place by sound, light, heat, and electric currents.
description_plain: Your child can watch and describe how energy moves from one place to another through sound, light, heat, and electricity.

subject: Science
description: Use evidence to construct an explanation relating the speed of an object to the energy of that object.
description_plain: Your child will use evidence to explain how the speed of something relates to its energy.

subject: Science
description: Plan and carry out fair tests in which variables are controlled and failure points are considered to identify aspects of a model or prototype that can be improved.
description_plain: Your child can plan and do fair tests by controlling variables and thinking about what might go wrong to find ways to improve a model or prototype.

subject: Science
description: Generate and compare multiple possible solutions to a problem based on how well each is likely to meet the criteria and constraints of the problem.
description_plain: Your child can come up with and compare several possible solutions to a problem to see which ones best meet the goals and limits.

subject: Science
description: Define a simple design problem reflecting a need or a want that includes specified criteria for success and constraints on materials, time, or cost.
description_plain: Your child can describe a simple design problem based on a need or want, including what success looks like and limits like materials, time, or cost.

subject: Science
description: Represent data in graphical displays to reveal patterns of daily changes in length and direction of shadows, day and night, daily appearance of the moon, and the seasonal appearance of some stars in the night sky.
description_plain: Your child can create graphs to show daily changes in shadows, day and night, and how some stars appear differently during seasons.

subject: Science
description: Support an argument that differences in the apparent brightness of the sun compared to other stars is due to their relative distances from the Earth.
description_plain: Your child can explain why the sun looks brighter than other stars because it is closer to Earth.

subject: Science
description: Support an argument that the gravitational force exerted by Earth on objects is directed toward the center of the Earth.
description_plain: Your child can explain that Earth's gravity pulls things toward its center.

subject: Science
description: Obtain and combine information about ways individual communities use science ideas to protect the Earth's resources and environment.
description_plain: Your child can find and combine information about how communities use science to protect Earth's resources and environment.

subject: Science
description: Describe and graph the amounts of salt water and fresh water in various reservoirs to provide evidence about the distribution of water on Earth.
description_plain: Your child can describe and make a graph showing how much salt water and fresh water are in different places on Earth.

subject: Science
description: Develop a model using an example to describe ways the geosphere, biosphere, hydrosphere (water), cryosphere (ice), and/or atmosphere interact.
description_plain: Your child can make a model to show how Earth's land, water, ice, air, and living things all work together.

subject: Science
description: Develop and describe a model that describes the movement of matter among plants, animals, decomposers, and the environment.
description_plain: Your child can make and explain a model that shows how matter moves between plants, animals, decomposers, and the environment.

subject: Science
description: Support an argument that plants get the materials they need for growth chiefly from air and water.
description_plain: Your child can explain that plants get most of what they need to grow from air and water.

subject: Science
description: Use models to describe that energy in animals' food (used for body repair, growth, and motion and to maintain body warmth) was once energy from the sun.
description_plain: Your child can use models to show that the energy animals get from food originally comes from the sun.

subject: Science
description: Conduct an investigation to determine whether the mixing of two or more substances results in new substances.
description_plain: Your child can do an experiment to find out if mixing substances creates new substances.

subject: Science
description: Make observations and measurements to identify materials based on their properties.
description_plain: Your child can observe and measure materials to identify them by their properties.

subject: Science
description: Measure and graph quantities to provide evidence that regardless of the type of change that occurs when heating, cooling, or mixing substances, the total weight of matter is conserved.
description_plain: Your child can measure and graph amounts to show that matter's weight stays the same during heating, cooling, or mixing.

subject: Science
description: Develop and use a model to describe that matter is made of particles too small to be seen.
description_plain: Your child can make and use a model to show that everything is made of tiny particles too small to see.

subject: Science
description: Develop a model to generate data for repetitive testing and modification of a proposed object, tool, or process such that an optimal design can be achieved.
description_plain: Your child can create a model to test and improve a tool or process many times to find the best design.

subject: Science
description: Develop a model to generate data for repetitive testing and modification of a proposed object, tool, or process such that an optimal design can be achieved.
description_plain: Your child can create a model to test and improve a tool or process many times to find the best design.

subject: Science
description: Develop a model to generate data for repetitive testing and modification of a proposed object, tool, or process such that an optimal design can be achieved.
description_plain: Your child can create a model to test and improve a tool or process many times to find the best design.

subject: Science
description: Analyze data from tests to determine similarities and differences among several design solutions to identify the best characteristics of each that can be combined into a new solution to better meet the criteria for success.
description_plain: Your child can study test results to find the best parts of different designs and combine them to make a better solution.

subject: Science
description: Analyze data from tests to determine similarities and differences among several design solutions to identify the best characteristics of each that can be combined into a new solution to better meet the criteria for success.
description_plain: Your child can study test results to find the best parts of different designs and combine them to make a better solution.

subject: Science
description: Analyze data from tests to determine similarities and differences among several design solutions to identify the best characteristics of each that can be combined into a new solution to better meet the criteria for success.
description_plain: Your child can study test results to find the best parts of different designs and combine them to make a better solution.

subject: Science
description: Evaluate competing design solutions using a systematic process to determine how well they meet the criteria and constraints of the problem.
description_plain: Your child can compare different design ideas carefully to see which one best meets the problem's needs and limits.

subject: Science
description: Evaluate competing design solutions using a systematic process to determine how well they meet the criteria and constraints of the problem.
description_plain: Your child can compare different design ideas carefully to see which one best meets the problem's needs and limits.

subject: Science
description: Evaluate competing design solutions using a systematic process to determine how well they meet the criteria and constraints of the problem.
description_plain: Your child can compare different design ideas carefully to see which one best meets the problem's needs and limits.

subject: Science
description: Define the criteria and constraints of a design problem with sufficient precision to ensure a successful solution, taking into account relevant scientific principles and potential impacts on people and the natural environment that may limit possible solutions.
description_plain: Your child can clearly describe the goals and limits of a design problem, considering science and how it affects people and the environment.

subject: Science
description: Define the criteria and constraints of a design problem with sufficient precision to ensure a successful solution, taking into account relevant scientific principles and potential impacts on people and the natural environment that may limit possible solutions.

subject: Science
description: Plan and conduct an investigation to provide evidence that the transfer of thermal energy when two components of different temperature are combined within a closed system results in a more uniform energy distribution among the components in the system (second law of thermodynamics).
